ai.description:
Registers the `luna.ai.*` game AI toolkit API.

Provides factory functions and UserData wrappers for AI subsystems:
AIWorld, Agent, Blackboard, StateMachine, BehaviorTree, SteeringManager,
PathGrid, FlowField, QLearner, UtilityAI, GOAPPlanner, InfluenceMap,
Squad, and CommandQueue.

methods.name: addAgent
methods.description: Registers a new agent named `name` in this world and returns its handle.
methods.full_doc:
Registers a new agent named `name` in this world and returns its handle.

# Parameters
- `name` — `string`: Unique identifier for the new agent.
# Returns
An `Agent` handle ready for further configuration.
methods.params_doc: - `name` — `string`: Unique identifier for the new agent.
methods.returns_doc: An `Agent` handle ready for further configuration.

methods.name: getAgent
methods.description: Looks up a registered agent by name.
methods.full_doc:
Looks up a registered agent by name.

# Parameters
- `name` — `string`: Name of the agent to retrieve.
# Returns
The `Agent` handle, or `nil` if no agent with that name exists.
methods.params_doc: - `name` — `string`: Name of the agent to retrieve.
methods.returns_doc: The `Agent` handle, or `nil` if no agent with that name exists.

methods.name: removeAgent
methods.description: Removes and destroys the given agent from this world.
methods.full_doc:
Removes and destroys the given agent from this world.

# Parameters
- `agent` — `Agent`: Handle of the agent to remove, obtained from `addAgent`.
methods.params_doc: - `agent` — `Agent`: Handle of the agent to remove, obtained from `addAgent`.

methods.name: getAgentCount
methods.description: Returns the number of agents currently registered in this world.
methods.full_doc:

`integer` — total agent count.

methods.full_doc:

methods.returns_doc: A `Blackboard` containing data visible to all agents in this world.

methods.name: update
methods.description: Advances all agents in the world by `dt` seconds, integrating velocity into position.
methods.full_doc:
Advances all agents in the world by `dt` seconds, integrating velocity into position.

# Parameters
- `dt` — `number`: Elapsed seconds since the last frame.
methods.params_doc: - `dt` — `number`: Elapsed seconds since the last frame.

Returns the unique name this agent was registered under.

methods.name: setPosition
methods.description: Teleports the agent to world-space coordinates (`x`, `y`).
methods.full_doc:

- `x` — `number`: Horizontal world-space position.

methods.description: Returns the agent's current world-space position.

Two numbers `x, y` representing world-space coordinates.

functions.name: newSource
functions.description: Loads an audio file and returns a source handle.
functions.full_doc: Loads an audio file and returns a source handle.

functions.name: play
functions.description: Plays the audio source from the beginning.
functions.full_doc: Plays the audio source from the beginning.

functions.name: stop
functions.description: Stops playback of the audio source.
functions.full_doc: Stops playback of the audio source.

functions.name: setVolume
functions.description: Sets the playback volume (0.0 - 1.0) of the source.
functions.full_doc: Sets the playback volume (0.0 - 1.0) of the source.

functions.name: getVolume
functions.description: Returns the current volume of the audio source.
functions.full_doc: Returns the current volume of the audio source.

functions.name: pause
functions.description: Pauses the audio source at its current position.
functions.full_doc: Pauses the audio source at its current position.

functions.name: resume
functions.description: Resumes playback of a paused audio source from its current position.
functions.full_doc:
Resumes playback of a paused audio source from its current position.

# Parameters
- `source` — Audio source ID to resume.
functions.params_doc: - `source` — Audio source ID to resume.

functions.description: Returns the current pitch multiplier of the source.
functions.full_doc: Returns the current pitch multiplier of the source.

functions.name: isPlaying
functions.description: Returns whether the audio source is currently playing.
functions.full_doc: Returns whether the audio source is currently playing.

functions.name: isPaused
functions.description: Returns whether the audio source is currently paused.
functions.full_doc: Returns whether the audio source is currently paused.

functions.name: isStopped
functions.description: Returns whether the audio source is stopped.
functions.full_doc: Returns whether the audio source is stopped.

functions.name: setLooping
functions.description: Enables or disables looping playback for the source.
functions.full_doc: Enables or disables looping playback for the source.

functions.name: isLooping
functions.description: Returns whether the audio source is set to loop.
functions.full_doc: Returns whether the audio source is set to loop.

functions.description: Plays the audio source in a continuous loop.
functions.full_doc: Plays the audio source in a continuous loop.

functions.description: Sets the stereo panning (-1.0 left to 1.0 right) of the source.
functions.full_doc: Sets the stereo panning (-1.0 left to 1.0 right) of the source.

functions.description: Returns the current stereo panning of the source.

functions.description: Sets the global master volume (0.0 - 1.0).
functions.full_doc: Sets the global master volume (0.0 - 1.0).

functions.description: Returns the current master volume scale (0.0 - 1.0) applied to all audio output.

debug.description:
Registers the `luna.devtools.*` runtime diagnostics and developer tools API.

Five subsystems: structured logger, hierarchical profiler, frame statistics,
file watcher, and Lua debug bridge. All module-level functions — no
registered types.

functions.description: Log at any level with optional source/line info.
functions.full_doc: Log at any level with optional source/line info.

functions.name: setLogLevel
functions.description: Sets the minimum log level to record.
functions.full_doc: Sets the minimum log level to record.

functions.name: getLogLevel
functions.description: Returns the current minimum log level.
functions.full_doc: Returns the current minimum log level.

functions.description: Toggles stderr console output.
functions.full_doc: Toggles stderr console output.

functions.name: getLogFile

functions.full_doc: Returns the current log file path.

functions.full_doc: Returns whether the profiler is enabled.

functions.full_doc: Ends the most recent timing zone.

functions.full_doc: Seals the current frame of profiling data.

functions.full_doc: Returns the number of retained profile frames.

functions.full_doc: Returns zone data for a specific frame (0 = most recent).

functions.full_doc: Scans watched files and returns paths whose modification time changed.

debugbridge.description:
Registers the `luna.debugbridge.*` TCP debug server API.

Embeds a JSON-over-TCP server (127.0.0.1 only) inside the running game.
External tools (VS Code extension, MCP server) connect to inspect and
control the game at runtime. Network I/O runs on a background thread;
Lua-dependent methods are queued and dispatched via `poll()` on the main thread.

functions.name: start
functions.description: Start the TCP debug server on 127.0.0.1:port.
functions.full_doc: Start the TCP debug server on 127.0.0.1:port.

functions.name: stop
functions.description: Stop the TCP debug server and close all connections.
functions.full_doc: Stop the TCP debug server and close all connections.

functions.name: isRunning
functions.description: Returns whether the server is currently running.
functions.full_doc: Returns whether the server is currently running.

functions.name: getPort
functions.description: Returns the server port (0 if not running).
functions.full_doc: Returns the server port (0 if not running).

functions.name: getClientCount
functions.description: Returns the number of connected TCP clients.
functions.full_doc: Returns the number of connected TCP clients.

functions.name: poll
functions.description: Poll for pending Lua-dependent requests from TCP clients.
functions.full_doc:
Poll for pending Lua-dependent requests from TCP clients.
Must be called each frame from luna.update().

functions.description: Captures a print message and broadcasts it to connected clients.

functions.full_doc: Sets the maximum print history size.

functions.name: broadcast
functions.description: Broadcasts a JSON event to all connected clients.
functions.full_doc: Broadcasts a JSON event to all connected clients.

graphics_ext.description:
Lua API bindings for Phase 24 graphics extension types.

Provides UserData wrappers and factory functions for extended graphics
subsystems: Light2D, TextureAtlas, DrawLayer, Viewport, ViewportScale,
SpriteSheet, PolygonMap, GraphRenderer, LargeMapRenderer, ColumnBatch,
Camera2D, Animation, Trail, DecalSurface, and PaletteLUT.

localization.description:
Registers the `luna.localization.*` internationalization API.

Provides a structured translation system supporting multiple languages,
fallback chains, pluralization, variable interpolation, and language-change
callbacks.

log.description:
Structured game-level logging API (`luna.log.*`).

Exposes convenience logging functions so Lua scripts can emit messages at
specific severity levels and query or change the active log level at
runtime without going through the `luna.system` namespace.

functions.description: Emit a debug-severity log message from Lua.
functions.full_doc:
Emit a debug-severity log message from Lua.

# Parameters
- `message` — The message string to log.
functions.params_doc: - `message` — The message string to log.

functions.params_doc: - `message` — The message string to log.

functions.name: print
functions.description: Emit a log message from Lua at the specified severity level.
functions.full_doc:
Emit a log message from Lua at the specified severity level.

Accepts the same level strings as `luna.log.setLevel`:
`"debug"`, `"info"`, `"warn"`, `"error"`, `"trace"`.
Unknown levels fall back to `info`.
# Parameters
- `level` — Severity level string.
- `message` — The message string to log.
functions.params_doc:
- `level` — Severity level string.
- `message` — The message string to log.

functions.name: setLevel
functions.description: Set the minimum severity level for runtime log messages.
functions.full_doc:

scene.description:
Registers the `luna.scene.*` scene stack, registry, data store, and depth-sorter API.

The scene stack manages a LIFO stack of Lua scene tables with optional
visual transitions. Scene tables are stored via `mlua::RegistryKey` and
identified by `SceneId`. Inter-scene data is stored similarly.

The `DepthSorter` UserData batches draw callbacks by depth for ordered
compositing each frame.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Registers a draw callback at the given depth layer. Higher `depth` values draw in front.
methods.full_doc:
Registers a draw callback at the given depth layer. Higher `depth` values draw in front.

# Parameters
- `callback` — `function`: Draw callback `function()` called when flushing this layer.
- `depth` — `number`: Depth value determining draw order (lower = drawn first).
methods.params_doc:
- `callback` — `function`: Draw callback `function()` called when flushing this layer.
- `depth` — `number`: Depth value determining draw order (lower = drawn first).

methods.name: addObject
methods.description: Registers a table object with a `draw` method at the given depth.
methods.full_doc:
Registers a table object with a `draw` method at the given depth.

# Parameters
- `obj` — `table`: Object with a `draw()` method. Uses `obj.depth` if no explicit depth is provided.
methods.params_doc: - `obj` — `table`: Object with a `draw()` method. Uses `obj.depth` if no explicit depth is provided.

methods.name: sort
methods.description: Sorts all registered callbacks and objects by their depth values (ascending).
methods.full_doc: Sorts all registered callbacks and objects by their depth values (ascending).

methods.name: flush
methods.description: Calls all registered draw callbacks and object `draw()` methods in sorted depth order, then clears the list.
methods.full_doc: Calls all registered draw callbacks and object `draw()` methods in sorted depth order, then clears the list.

methods.name: clear
methods.description: Removes all registered callbacks and objects without calling them.
methods.full_doc: Removes all registered callbacks and objects without calling them.

methods.name: getCount
methods.description: Returns the number of callbacks and objects currently registered.
methods.full_doc:
Returns the number of callbacks and objects currently registered.

# Returns
`integer` — number of registered draw entries.

sound.description: Registers the `luna.sound.*` decoded audio sample manipulation API.

functions.name: newSoundData
functions.description: Creates a raw PCM audio buffer for building procedurally generated sound.
functions.full_doc:
Creates a raw PCM audio buffer for building procedurally generated sound.

# Parameters
- `samples` — Total number of PCM samples to allocate.
- `sampleRate` — Sample rate in Hz (e.g. 44100).
- `bitDepth` — Bit depth per sample (8 or 16).
- `channels` — Number of channels (1 = mono, 2 = stereo).
# Returns
New SoundData object ID.
functions.params_doc:
- `samples` — Total number of PCM samples to allocate.
- `sampleRate` — Sample rate in Hz (e.g. 44100).
- `bitDepth` — Bit depth per sample (8 or 16).
- `channels` — Number of channels (1 = mono, 2 = stereo).
functions.returns_doc: New SoundData object ID.

thread.description:
Registers the `luna.thread` namespace.

Provides Lua-level multithreading: create background threads that run
independent Lua VMs and communicate via thread-safe channels.

functions.name: newThread
functions.description: Create a new background thread from a Lua code string.
functions.full_doc:
Create a new background thread from a Lua code string.
The thread gets its own Lua VM and can communicate via channels.

functions.name: newChannel
functions.description: Create an unnamed thread-safe channel for inter-thread communication.
functions.full_doc: Create an unnamed thread-safe channel for inter-thread communication.

functions.name: getChannel
functions.description: Get or create a named global channel (singleton, shared across threads).
functions.full_doc: Get or create a named global channel (singleton, shared across threads).

methods.name: start
methods.description: Launches the background thread, passing optional arguments to the Lua script via `...`.
methods.full_doc:
Launches the background thread, passing optional arguments to the Lua script via `...`.

# Parameters
- `...` — `any`: Optional arguments forwarded to the thread script as Lua values.
methods.params_doc: - `...` — `any`: Optional arguments forwarded to the thread script as Lua values.

methods.name: wait
methods.description: Blocks the calling coroutine until this thread finishes execution.
methods.full_doc: Blocks the calling coroutine until this thread finishes execution.

methods.name: isRunning
methods.description: Returns `true` if the thread is currently executing.
methods.full_doc:
Returns `true` if the thread is currently executing.

# Returns
`boolean`.

methods.name: getError
methods.description: Returns the error message if the thread terminated with a Lua error, or `nil` if it completed normally.
methods.full_doc:
Returns the error message if the thread terminated with a Lua error, or `nil` if it completed normally.

# Returns
`string` — error message, or `nil`.

tilemap.description:
Registers the `luna.tilemap.*` tile map, tileset, autotile, and procedural generation API.

Exposes `LuaTileSet`, `LuaTileMap`, `LuaAutoTileSheet`, `LuaMapBlock`, `LuaMapGroup`,
`LuaMapScript`, and `LuaMapGen` UserData types wrapping the `crate::tilemap` module.

All Lua-facing tile/layer/segment indices are **1-based**; Rust internals are 0-based.

functions.name: getDelta
functions.description: Returns the delta time (seconds) for the current frame.
functions.full_doc: Returns the delta time (seconds) for the current frame.

functions.name: getFPS
functions.description: Returns the measured frames-per-second for the current frame.
functions.full_doc: Returns the measured frames-per-second for the current frame.

functions.name: getTime
functions.description: Returns the total elapsed time in seconds since engine start.
functions.full_doc: Returns the total elapsed time in seconds since engine start.

functions.name: getAverageDelta
functions.description: Returns the rolling-average frame delta time in seconds.
functions.full_doc: Returns the rolling-average frame delta time in seconds.

functions.name: step
functions.description: Advances the timer by one step (called automatically each frame).
functions.full_doc: Advances the timer by one step (called automatically each frame).

functions.name: getMicroTime
functions.description: Returns the high-resolution monotonic timer value in microseconds.
functions.full_doc:
Returns the high-resolution monotonic timer value in microseconds.

# Returns
Elapsed microseconds as a 64-bit integer.
functions.returns_doc: Elapsed microseconds as a 64-bit integer.

functions.name: sleep
functions.description: Suspends execution for the given number of seconds.
functions.full_doc: Suspends execution for the given number of seconds.

functions.name: newScheduler
functions.description: Creates an independent timer scheduler for managing a set of callbacks.
functions.full_doc:
Creates an independent timer scheduler for managing a set of callbacks.

# Returns
New Scheduler object with its own after/every/update interface.
functions.returns_doc: New Scheduler object with its own after/every/update interface.

methods.name: after
methods.description: Calls a Lua function after the given delay in seconds.
methods.full_doc: Calls a Lua function after the given delay in seconds.

methods.name: cancel
methods.full_doc: Cancels a scheduled timer callback.

methods.name: cancelNamed
methods.description: Cancels a specific named timer that was scheduled on this scheduler.
methods.full_doc:
Cancels a specific named timer that was scheduled on this scheduler.

# Parameters
- `name` — Name string used when the timer was created.
methods.params_doc: - `name` — Name string used when the timer was created.

methods.name: cancelAll
methods.description: Cancels and removes every pending timer entry from this scheduler.
methods.full_doc: Cancels and removes every pending timer entry from this scheduler.

methods.name: pause
methods.description: Pauses the scheduler so no pending callbacks fire until resumed.
methods.full_doc: Pauses the scheduler so no pending callbacks fire until resumed.

methods.name: resume
methods.description: Resumes a paused scheduler, allowing its callbacks to fire again.
methods.full_doc: Resumes a paused scheduler, allowing its callbacks to fire again.

methods.name: isPaused
methods.description: Returns whether the scheduler is currently paused and not advancing timers.
methods.full_doc:
Returns whether the scheduler is currently paused and not advancing timers.

# Returns
true if paused, false if running.
methods.returns_doc: true if paused, false if running.

methods.name: getRemaining
methods.description: Returns the time remaining before the next invocation of the given timer.
methods.full_doc:
Returns the time remaining before the next invocation of the given timer.

# Parameters
- `id` — Timer ID returned when the timer was created.
# Returns
Remaining time in seconds.
methods.params_doc: - `id` — Timer ID returned when the timer was created.

methods.name: getInterval
methods.description: Returns the repeat interval of the given timer entry in seconds.
methods.full_doc:
Returns the repeat interval of the given timer entry in seconds.

# Parameters
- `id` — Timer ID returned when the timer was created.
# Returns
Interval in seconds, or nil for one-shot timers.
methods.params_doc: - `id` — Timer ID returned when the timer was created.
methods.returns_doc: Interval in seconds, or nil for one-shot timers.

methods.name: getRepeatCount
methods.description: Returns how many times the given timer has fired since it was created.
methods.full_doc:
Returns how many times the given timer has fired since it was created.

# Parameters
- `id` — Timer ID.
# Returns
Fire count as an integer.

methods.name: getCount
methods.description: Returns the number of active timer entries currently in the scheduler.
methods.full_doc:
Returns the number of active timer entries currently in the scheduler.

# Returns
Active timer count as an integer.
methods.returns_doc: Active timer count as an integer.

methods.name: isEmpty
methods.description: Returns whether the scheduler has no active timer entries.
methods.full_doc:
Returns whether the scheduler has no active timer entries.

# Returns
true if the scheduler queue is empty.
methods.returns_doc: true if the scheduler queue is empty.

methods.name: setInterval
methods.description: Changes the repeat interval of an existing timer without recreating it.
methods.full_doc:
Changes the repeat interval of an existing timer without recreating it.

# Parameters
- `id` — Timer ID.
- `interval` — New interval in seconds.
methods.params_doc:
- `id` — Timer ID.
- `interval` — New interval in seconds.

methods.name: resetEvent
methods.description: Resets the elapsed time of the given timer so it fires again after its full interval.
methods.full_doc:
Resets the elapsed time of the given timer so it fires again after its full interval.

# Parameters
- `id` — Timer ID to reset.

methods.name: setTimeScale
methods.description: Sets a time-scale factor that speeds up or slows down all timers in the scheduler.
methods.full_doc:
Sets a time-scale factor that speeds up or slows down all timers in the scheduler.

# Parameters
- `scale` — Time multiplier (1.0 = real time, 2.0 = double speed).
methods.params_doc: - `scale` — Time multiplier (1.0 = real time, 2.0 = double speed).

methods.name: getTimeScale
methods.description: Returns the current time-scale factor applied to all timers in this scheduler.
methods.full_doc:
Returns the current time-scale factor applied to all timers in this scheduler.

# Returns
Time scale as a number (1.0 = real time, 0.5 = half speed).
methods.returns_doc: Time scale as a number (1.0 = real time, 0.5 = half speed).

methods.name: update
methods.description: Advances all pending timers by dt seconds.
methods.full_doc: Advances all pending timers by dt seconds.

functions.name: setTitle
functions.description: Sets the text displayed in the window's title bar.
functions.full_doc:
Sets the text displayed in the window's title bar.

# Parameters
- `title` — New title string to display.
functions.params_doc: - `title` — New title string to display.

functions.name: getTitle
functions.description: Returns the current text displayed in the operating-system window title bar.
functions.full_doc:
Returns the current text displayed in the operating-system window title bar.

# Returns
Title string.
functions.returns_doc: Title string.

functions.full_doc: Returns the window width in pixels.

functions.full_doc: Returns the window height in pixels.

functions.full_doc: Returns the window dimensions (width, height).

functions.description: Enables or disables fullscreen mode.
functions.full_doc: Enables or disables fullscreen mode.

Returns whether the window has been created and is not yet closed.

functions.full_doc: Returns whether the mouse cursor is inside the window.

ai::agent.module_doc: Autonomous agent with kinematic state and attached decision subsystems.

items.name: DecisionModel
items.description: Controls which subsystems are ticked during `AIWorld::update`.  # Variants - `Fsm` — Fsm variant. - `Bt` — Bt variant. - `Steering` — Steering variant. - `FsmSteering` — FsmSteering variant. - `BtSteering` — BtSteering variant.

items.name: Agent
items.description: Autonomous agent with kinematic state and attached decision subsystems.  # Fields - `name` — `String`. - `priority` — `i32`. - `position` — `(f32, f32)`. - `velocity` — `(f32, f32)`. - `max_speed` — `f32`. - `max_force` — `f32`. - `decision_model` — `DecisionModel`. - `blackboard` — `Blackboard`. - `tags` — `HashSet<String>`. - `fsm_index` — `Option<usize>`. - `bt_index` — `Option<usize>`. - `steering_index` — `Option<usize>`.  Created via `AIWorld::add_agent()`. Carries position, velocity, kinematic constraints, an optional FSM/BT/SteeringManager, and a local Blackboard.

ai::behavior_tree.module_doc: Behavior Tree with composite, decorator, and leaf nodes.  All nodes return `BTStatus`. Composites memo the running-child index to support multi-frame "running" states.

items.name: BTStatus
items.description: Return status produced by every BT node after a tick.  # Variants - `Success` — Success variant. - `Failure` — Failure variant. - `Running` — Running variant.

items.name: ParallelPolicy
items.description: Parallel node success/failure policy.  # Variants - `RequireOne` — RequireOne variant. - `RequireAll` — RequireAll variant.

items.name: BTNode
items.description: Recursive behavior-tree node.  # Variants - `Selector` — Selector variant. - `Sequence` — Sequence variant. - `Parallel` — Parallel variant. - `Inverter` — Inverter variant. - `Repeater` — Repeater variant. - `Succeeder` — Succeeder variant. - `Action` — Action variant. - `Condition` — Condition variant.

items.name: BehaviorTree
items.description: Root container holding the behavior tree and caching the last tick status.  # Fields - `root` — `Option<BTNode>`. - `last_status` — `BTStatus`.

ai::blackboard.module_doc: Typed key-value store with optional parent chain for hierarchical lookup.

items.name: BlackboardValue
items.description: Typed value stored in a Blackboard slot.  # Variants - `Number` — Number variant. - `Bool` — Bool variant. - `Text` — Text variant.

items.name: Blackboard
items.description: Key-value store with optional parent chain for hierarchical lookup.  # Fields - `entries` — `HashMap<String, BlackboardValue>`. - `parent` — `Option<Box<Blackboard>>`.  Used by agents, squads, and worlds to share named data. `get*` walks the parent chain; `set*` writes locally only.

ai::command_queue.module_doc: RTS-style ordered command queue for a single unit.

items.name: Command
items.description: A single RTS unit command with metadata.  # Fields - `kind` — `String`. - `callback` — `RegistryKey`. - `target_x` — `f32`. - `target_y` — `f32`. - `priority` — `i32`. - `interruptible` — `bool`.

items.name: CommandQueue
items.description: RTS-style ordered command queue.  # Fields - `commands` — `VecDeque<Command>`.  Commands are dequeued from the front. Three insertion modes: `enqueue` (append), `push_front` (interrupt), `replace` (clear + enqueue).

ai::flowfield.module_doc: BFS flow field built over a PathGrid.

items.name: FlowField
items.description: BFS flow field that stores normalized direction vectors toward a goal.  # Fields - `width` — `usize`. - `height` — `usize`. - `goal` — `Option<(usize, usize)>`.  Built from a PathGrid. Each cell stores a direction vector and BFS distance.

ai::fsm.module_doc: Finite State Machine with priority-ordered guarded transitions.

items.name: StateCallbacks
items.description: Lua lifecycle hooks for a single FSM state.  # Fields - `on_enter` — `Option<RegistryKey>`. - `on_update` — `Option<RegistryKey>`. - `on_exit` — `Option<RegistryKey>`.

items.name: Transition
items.description: Guarded edge in the state graph.  # Fields - `from` — `String`. - `to` — `String`. - `guard` — `Option<RegistryKey>`. - `priority` — `i32`.

items.name: StateMachine
items.description: Finite State Machine with priority-ordered guarded transitions.  # Fields - `states` — `HashMap<String, StateCallbacks>`. - `transitions` — `Vec<Transition>`. - `current_state` — `Option<String>`. - `initial_state` — `Option<String>`. - `time_in_state` — `f32`.  States have optional enter/update/exit Lua callbacks. Transitions are checked in descending priority order each frame.

ai::goap.module_doc: A★ planner over boolean world state for multi-step tactical planning.

items.name: GOAPAction
items.description: A single GOAP action with boolean preconditions and effects.  # Fields - `name` — `String`. - `cost` — `f64`. - `callback` — `Option<RegistryKey>`. - `preconditions` — `HashMap<String, bool>`. - `effects` — `HashMap<String, bool>`.

items.name: GOAPGoal
items.description: A planning goal expressed as a target boolean world state.  # Fields - `name` — `String`. - `priority` — `f64`. - `state` — `HashMap<String, bool>`.

items.name: GOAPPlanner
items.description: A★ planner over boolean world state for multi-step tactical planning.  # Fields - `actions` — `Vec<GOAPAction>`. - `goals` — `Vec<GOAPGoal>`.

ai::influence_map.module_doc: Multi-layer spatial float grid for strategic area analysis.

items.name: InfluenceMap
items.description: Multi-layer spatial float grid for influence mapping.  # Fields - `width` — `usize`. - `height` — `usize`. - `cell_size` — `f32`. - `layers` — `HashMap<String, Vec<f32>>`.  Stores named float layers of shape width × height. Supports stamping, propagation (diffusion), decay, and spatial queries.

ai.module_doc: Game AI toolkit: FSM, Behavior Trees, Steering, Pathfinding, Q-Learning, and more.  All subsystems are decoupled: agents can be driven by FSM, BT, SteeringManager, or any mix. All computation is pure CPU math — no GPU, audio, or window access required.

items.name: agent
items.description: Autonomous agent with pluggable decision models.

items.name: behavior_tree
items.description: Behavior tree nodes (sequence, selector, parallel, decorators).

items.name: blackboard
items.description: Shared key-value store for inter-agent communication.

items.name: command_queue
items.description: Queue-based action scheduling with undo support.

items.name: flowfield
items.description: Dijkstra-based flow field for crowd pathfinding.

items.name: fsm
items.description: Finite state machine with named states and transitions.

items.name: goap
items.description: Goal-oriented action planning (GOAP) solver.

items.name: influence_map
items.description: Grid-based influence map for spatial reasoning.

items.name: pathgrid
items.description: Weighted grid pathfinding (A*, Dijkstra) with obstacle support.

items.name: qlearner
items.description: Tabular Q-learning agent for reinforcement learning.

items.name: squad
items.description: Squad coordination: formation, morale, and group behaviour.

items.name: steering
items.description: Steering behaviours (seek, flee, wander, flocking, etc.).

items.name: utility_ai
items.description: Utility-AI scorer with weighted action selection.

items.name: world
items.description: Spatial AI world with team assignment and awareness radius.

ai::pathgrid.module_doc: A★ navigation grid for pathfinding.  Internal coordinates are 0-based. Lua-facing coordinates are 1-based (conversion happens in the API binding layer).

items.name: Cell
items.description: Single cell of the navigation grid.  # Fields - `walkable` — `bool`. - `cost` — `f32`.

items.name: PathGrid
items.description: A★ navigation grid.  # Fields - `width` — `usize`. - `height` — `usize`. - `cell_size` — `f32`.  Cells are stored row-major, 0-based internally. Supports walkability toggling and per-cell cost multipliers.

items.name: QLearner
items.description: Tabular epsilon-greedy Q-learner for reinforcement learning.  # Fields - `state_count` — `usize`. - `action_count` — `usize`. - `qtable` — `Vec<f64>`. - `alpha` — `f64`. - `gamma` — `f64`. - `epsilon` — `f64`. - `epsilon_decay` — `f64`. - `episode_count` — `u64`.  Q-table is a flat `Vec<f64>` indexed as `state * action_count + action`. Uses Bellman update: Q(s,a) ← Q(s,a) + α\[r + γ max_a' Q(s',a') - Q(s,a)\]

ai::squad.module_doc: Multi-agent formation group with offset computation.

items.name: FormationType
items.description: Formation shapes for squad positioning.  # Variants - `None` — None variant. - `Line` — Line variant. - `Wedge` — Wedge variant. - `Circle` — Circle variant. - `Column` — Column variant.

items.name: Squad
items.description: Multi-agent formation group.  # Fields - `name` — `String`. - `members` — `Vec<String>`. - `leader` — `Option<String>`. - `formation` — `FormationType`. - `formation_spacing` — `f32`. - `blackboard` — `Blackboard`.  Computes formation offset positions relative to the leader's position.

ai::steering.module_doc: Reynolds-style steering behaviors with weighted/priority combination.

items.name: Force
items.description: 2D force vector (fx, fy).

items.name: CombineMode
items.description: How multiple steering forces are combined.  # Variants - `Weighted` — Weighted variant. - `Priority` — Priority variant.

items.name: SteeringBase
items.description: Common data for all steering behaviors.  # Fields - `weight` — `f32`. - `enabled` — `bool`.

items.name: SteeringBehaviorType
items.description: Enumeration of all concrete steering behavior types.  # Variants - `Seek` — Seek variant. - `Flee` — Flee variant. - `Arrive` — Arrive variant. - `Wander` — Wander variant. - `Pursue` — Pursue variant. - `Evade` — Evade variant. - `Flock` — Flock variant.

items.name: SteeringManager
items.description: Manages a list of steering behaviors and combines their forces each frame.  # Fields - `behaviors` — `Vec<SteeringBehaviorType>`. - `combine_mode` — `CombineMode`. - `last_force` — `Force`.

ai::utility_ai.module_doc: Multi-axis utility scorer that chooses the action with highest composite score.

items.name: ResponseCurve
items.description: Response curve shapes for consideration scoring.  # Variants - `Linear` — Linear variant. - `Quadratic` — Quadratic variant. - `Logistic` — Logistic variant. - `Logit` — Logit variant. - `Step` — Step variant.

items.name: Consideration
items.description: A single axis of evaluation within a UtilityAI action.  # Fields - `name` — `String`. - `callback` — `RegistryKey`. - `curve` — `ResponseCurve`. - `p1` — `f64`. - `p2` — `f64`. - `p3` — `f64`. - `weight` — `f64`.

items.name: UAAction
items.description: One scored option in a UtilityAI decision space.  # Fields - `name` — `String`. - `scorer` — `RegistryKey`. - `considerations` — `Vec<Consideration>`. - `momentum_bonus` — `f64`.

items.name: UtilityAI
items.description: Multi-axis utility scorer; chooses the action with highest composite score.  # Fields - `actions` — `Vec<UAAction>`. - `last_action` — `Option<usize>`. - `last_scores` — `Vec<f64>`.

ai::world.module_doc: Container that owns agents and ticks them in descending priority order each frame.

items.name: AIWorld
items.description: Container that owns agents and ticks them each frame.  # Fields - `agents` — `Vec<Agent>`. - `name_index` — `HashMap<String, usize>`. - `global_blackboard` — `Blackboard`.  Agents are stored in insertion order with a name→index HashMap for O(1) lookup. `update(dt)` ticks all agents in descending priority order.

audio::bus.module_doc: Named audio bus for grouping sources under shared volume, pitch, and pause controls.

items.name: Bus
items.description: A named audio bus that applies volume, pitch, and pause overrides to assigned sources.  Buses are pure data containers — they hold no rodio resources. The `Mixer` multiplies a source's per-source volume/pitch by its assigned bus values. Setting a bus to paused suppresses playback of all assigned sources.

audio::midi.module_doc: MIDI SoundFont state management.  Provides `MidiState` for tracking whether a SoundFont (SF2) file has been loaded for MIDI instrument rendering.

items.name: MidiState
items.description: MIDI SoundFont state.  Tracks whether a SoundFont (SF2) file has been loaded and stores its raw bytes for future MIDI decoding.

audio::midi_player.module_doc: Software MIDI synthesizer: parses MIDI with `midly`, renders to PCM via sine-additive synthesis, and plays through a rodio `Sink`.

items.name: MidiData
items.description: Pre-parsed MIDI metadata extracted during `load()`.  # Fields - `duration_secs` — `f64`. - `ticks_per_beat` — `u16`. - `original_tempo_bpm` — `f64`. - `track_count` — `usize`. - `track_names` — `Vec<Option<String>>`. - `note_count` — `usize`. - `channel_count` — `usize`.

items.name: MidiPlayer
items.description: Software MIDI player with sine-additive synthesis.  Parses MIDI via `midly`, renders all tracks to a PCM buffer on `play()`, and feeds the result into a `rodio::Sink`. Supports per-channel volume, muting, track muting, tempo scaling, and looping.

audio::mixer.module_doc: Core audio mixer that owns every loaded sound and drives playback through rodio.  [`Mixer`] is the single point of entry for all audio operations in Luna2D.  It opens the system's default output stream on construction (via rodio) and maintains a slot-map of [`AudioEntry`] records — one per loaded sound.  Each entry embeds a `rodio::Sink` for playback control and cached metadata (duration, fade parameters, loop flag, filters).  # Source types  The mixer supports two loading strategies selected at load time: - **Static** — the raw file bytes are decoded and held in an `Arc<Vec<u8>>` inside the   entry.  Playback restarts by re-decoding from that in-memory buffer, giving very low   latency at the cost of higher RAM usage.  Best for short sound effects. - **Stream** — the file is opened fresh from disk on each play call and decoded   incrementally.  Lower memory overhead for long music tracks.  # Bus routing  Every sound can be assigned to a named audio bus stored in a secondary slot-map. Volume and pitch for a playing source are the product of the per-source values and the values of its assigned bus.  Setting a bus to paused suspends every source on that bus.  # Master volume  A single master-volume scalar is applied on top of all per-source and per-bus volumes, letting game code provide a global volume slider without touching individual sources.  # Time tracking  Because rodio `Sink` does not expose a playback position, the mixer tracks play-start instants and accumulated pre-pause seconds manually so `luna.audio.getTime` can return the current position in the stream.

items.name: SourceType
items.description: Type of audio source.  # Variants - `Static` — Static variant. - `Stream` — Stream variant.

items.name: PlayState
items.description: Playback state of an audio source.  # Variants - `Stopped` — Stopped variant. - `Playing` — Playing variant. - `Paused` — Paused variant.

items.name: Mixer
items.description: Manages audio output via rodio: loads sources, controls playback, and adjusts volume.  Supports static (in-memory) and streaming source types, per-source volume/pitch/pan, master volume, looping, batch operations, and source cloning.

audio.module_doc: Audio subsystem for Luna2D games.  This module provides sound loading, playback, and volume management through a [`Mixer`] that wraps the [rodio](https://crates.io/crates/rodio) audio library. Game code accesses audio through the `luna.audio` Lua bindings in [`crate::lua_api`], which delegates all work to [`Mixer`] and [`Bus`] instances stored in `SharedState`.  # Key types  - [`Mixer`] — central audio mixer; manages all loaded sounds, their playback state,   volume, pitch, pan, looping, and fade effects. - [`Bus`] — named group that applies a shared volume and pause state to every source   assigned to it (e.g. a "music" bus or "sfx" bus). - [`AudioSource`] — lightweight handle for a loaded audio file, carrying the path and   default playback settings. - [`SourceType`] — controls whether a sound is decoded fully into memory (`Static`) or   read incrementally from disk during playback (`Stream`). - [`PlayState`] — the current playback status of a source: `Stopped`, `Playing`, or `Paused`.  # Design notes  Buses are pure data containers; the mixer multiplies source volume/pitch by bus values on every `set_volume` or `update` call.  Audio sources are tracked in a slot-map keyed by [`crate::engine::resource_keys::SoundKey`], giving O(1) lookup and safe handle invalidation when sources are released.

items.name: bus
items.description: Named audio bus for grouping sources under shared volume/pitch/pause controls.

items.name: midi_player
items.description: Software MIDI synthesizer with sine-additive PCM rendering.

items.name: mixer
items.description: Low-level audio mixer using rodio for playback and volume control.

items.name: source
items.description: Audio source handle holding path, volume, and loop settings.

items.name: add_scalar

items.name: mul_scalar

items.name: div
items.description: Element-wise division of two arrays (same shape and dtype).  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`. - `b` — `&NdArray`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.

items.name: div_scalar
items.description: Divide every element by a scalar.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`. - `s` — `f64`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.

items.name: pow_scalar
items.description: Raise every element to a scalar exponent.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`. - `exp` — `f64`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.

items.name: abs

items.name: neg

items.name: clamp

items.name: eq
items.description: Element-wise equality comparison of two arrays. Returns Float32 with 0/1.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`. - `b` — `&NdArray`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.

items.name: eq_scalar
items.description: Element-wise equality comparison against a scalar. Returns Float32.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`. - `s` — `f64`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.

items.name: neq

items.name: neq_scalar
items.description: Element-wise not-equal comparison against a scalar. Returns Float32.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`. - `s` — `f64`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.

items.name: gt
items.description: Element-wise greater-than comparison of two arrays. Returns Float32.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`. - `b` — `&NdArray`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.

items.name: gt_scalar
items.description: Element-wise greater-than comparison against a scalar. Returns Float32.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`. - `s` — `f64`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.

items.name: lt
items.description: Element-wise less-than comparison of two arrays. Returns Float32.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`. - `b` — `&NdArray`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.

items.name: lt_scalar
items.description: Element-wise less-than comparison against a scalar. Returns Float32.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`. - `s` — `f64`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.

items.name: gte
items.description: Element-wise greater-than-or-equal comparison of two arrays. Returns Float32.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`. - `b` — `&NdArray`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.

items.name: gte_scalar
items.description: Element-wise greater-than-or-equal comparison against a scalar. Returns Float32.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`. - `s` — `f64`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.

items.name: lte
items.description: Element-wise less-than-or-equal comparison of two arrays. Returns Float32.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`. - `b` — `&NdArray`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.

items.name: lte_scalar
items.description: Element-wise less-than-or-equal comparison against a scalar. Returns Float32.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`. - `s` — `f64`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.

items.name: threshold
items.description: Threshold mask: returns Float32 array with 1.0 where `a >= val`, 0.0 otherwise.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`. - `val` — `f64`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.

items.name: where_mask
items.description: Conditional selection: where `cond != 0`, choose from `a`; otherwise from `b`.  # Parameters - `cond` — `&NdArray`. - `a` — `&NdArray`. - `b` — `&NdArray`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.  All three arrays must have the same shape. `a` and `b` must have the same dtype. `cond` is read as Float32.

items.name: count_nonzero
items.description: Count the number of non-zero elements.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`.  # Returns `usize`.

items.name: argmin
items.description: Return the flat index of the minimum element (0-based).  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`.  # Returns `usize`.

items.name: argmax
items.description: Return the flat index of the maximum element (0-based).  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`.  # Returns `usize`.

items.name: any
items.description: Returns `true` if any element is non-zero.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`.  # Returns `bool`.

items.name: all
items.description: Returns `true` if all elements are non-zero.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`.  # Returns `bool`.

items.name: sum
items.description: Sum of all elements.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`.  # Returns `f64`.

items.name: mean
items.description: Mean of all elements.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`.  # Returns `f64`.

items.name: min_val

items.name: max_val
items.description: Maximum value across all elements.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`.  # Returns `f64`.

items.name: sum_axis

items.name: mean_axis
items.description: Mean along a given axis.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`. - `axis` — `usize`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.

items.name: min_axis
items.description: Minimum along a given axis.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`. - `axis` — `usize`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.

items.name: max_axis
items.description: Maximum along a given axis.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`. - `axis` — `usize`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.

items.name: reshape
items.description: Reshape an array to a new shape with the same total element count.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`. - `new_shape` — `&[usize]`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.  Returns a new array with data copied from the original.

items.name: transpose_2d
items.description: Transpose a 2D array (swap rows and columns).  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.

items.name: clone_array
items.description: Clone an array (convenience wrapper).  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`.  # Returns `NdArray`.

items.name: bitwise_and
items.description: Bitwise AND of two Int32 arrays.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`. - `b` — `&NdArray`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.

items.name: bitwise_or
items.description: Bitwise OR of two Int32 arrays.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`. - `b` — `&NdArray`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.

items.name: bitwise_xor
items.description: Bitwise XOR of two Int32 arrays.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`. - `b` — `&NdArray`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.

items.name: bitwise_not
items.description: Bitwise NOT of an Int32 array.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.

items.name: bitwise_lshift
items.description: Bitwise left shift of an Int32 array by `amount` bits.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`. - `amount` — `u32`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.

items.name: bitwise_rshift
items.description: Bitwise right shift (arithmetic) of an Int32 array by `amount` bits.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`. - `amount` — `u32`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.

compute::spatial.module_doc: 2D spatial operations and linear algebra for NdArray.

items.name: convolve2d
items.description: 2D convolution with zero-padding (same-size output).  # Parameters - `input` — `&NdArray`. - `kernel` — `&NdArray`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.  The kernel center is at `(kR/2, kC/2)`. Both input and kernel must be 2D arrays. Output shape equals input shape.

items.name: dilate
items.description: Morphological dilation with a Manhattan-diamond structuring element.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`. - `radius` — `usize`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.  For each cell, output is 1.0 if any cell within Manhattan distance `radius` is non-zero in the input, 0.0 otherwise. Input must be 2D.

items.name: erode
items.description: Morphological erosion with a Manhattan-diamond structuring element.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`. - `radius` — `usize`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.  For each cell, output is 1.0 only if all cells within Manhattan distance `radius` are non-zero in the input, 0.0 otherwise. Input must be 2D.

items.name: flood_fill
items.description: Flood fill using BFS with 4-connectivity.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`. - `row` — `usize`. - `col` — `usize`. - `val` — `f64`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.  Starting from `(row, col)`, fills all connected cells that have the same value as the starting cell with `val`. Returns a new array (does not mutate the original). Indices are 0-based.

items.name: get_region
items.description: Extract a rectangular sub-region from a 2D array.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`. - `row` — `usize`. - `col` — `usize`. - `sub_rows` — `usize`. - `sub_cols` — `usize`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.  Returns a new 2D array of shape `(sub_rows, sub_cols)` starting at `(row, col)`. All indices are 0-based.

items.name: set_region
items.description: Copy a source 2D array into a target 2D array at position `(row, col)`.  # Parameters - `a` — `&mut NdArray`. - `row` — `usize`. - `col` — `usize`. - `src` — `&NdArray`.  # Returns `Result<(), String>`.  Modifies `a` in-place. All indices are 0-based.

items.name: matmul
items.description: Matrix multiplication of two 2D arrays: (m,k) × (k,n) → (m,n).  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`. - `b` — `&NdArray`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.  Uses a naive triple loop. Both arrays must be 2D with compatible inner dimensions.

items.name: dot
items.description: Dot product of two 1D arrays (same length). Returns a scalar.  # Parameters - `a` — `&NdArray`. - `b` — `&NdArray`.  # Returns `Result<f64, String>`.
