items.description: Lua UserData wrapper for a single post-processing effect.

items.name: register
items.description: Registers the `luna.postfx.*` API.  # Parameters - `lua` — `&Lua`. - `luna` — `&LuaTable`.  # Returns `LuaResult<()>`.

lua_api::quest_api.module_doc: Lua API bindings for `luna.quest.*`.  Exposes quest creation, stage management, objective tracking, and journal entries through the `luna.quest` table.

items.name: LuaObjective
items.description: Lua UserData wrapper for a quest objective.

items.name: LuaQuestStage
items.description: Lua UserData wrapper for a quest stage.

items.name: LuaQuest
items.description: Lua UserData wrapper for a quest.

items.name: LuaQuestLog
items.description: Lua UserData wrapper for a quest log.

items.name: register
items.description: Register `luna.quest.*` API with the Lua VM.

lua_api::resource_api.module_doc: Lua API bindings for the `luna.resource.*` resource economy module.  Provides `ResourceManager` UserData for managing named resources with capacity, flow rates, decay, interest, conversions, and overflow policies.

items.name: LuaResourceManager
items.description: Lua UserData wrapper for a resource economy manager.

items.name: register
items.description: Registers the `luna.resource` module with the Lua VM.

items.name: register
items.description: Registers `luna.savegame.*` functions into the Lua VM.  # Parameters - `lua` — `&Lua`. - `luna` — `&LuaTable`. - `_state` — `Rc<RefCell<SharedState>>`.  # Returns `LuaResult<()>`.  Provides a slot-based save/load system with collectors, schema versioning, dirty tracking, and auto-save.

lua_api::scene_api.module_doc: Registers the `luna.scene.*` scene stack, registry, data store, and depth-sorter API.  The scene stack manages a LIFO stack of Lua scene tables with optional visual transitions. Scene tables are stored via `mlua::RegistryKey` and identified by `SceneId`. Inter-scene data is stored similarly.  The `DepthSorter` UserData batches draw callbacks by depth for ordered compositing each frame.

items.name: register
items.description: Registers the `luna.scene` table with scene stack, registry, data store,  # Parameters - `lua` — `&Lua`. - `luna` — `&LuaTable`.  # Returns `LuaResult<()>`. and depth-sorter factory functions.

lua_api::sound_api.module_doc: Registers the `luna.sound.*` decoded audio sample manipulation API.

items.name: register
items.description: Registers the `luna.sound` table on the provided `luna` namespace.  # Parameters - `lua` — `&Lua`. - `luna` — `&LuaTable`. - `state` — `Rc<RefCell<SharedState>>`.  # Returns `LuaResult<()>`.

lua_api::stats_api.module_doc: Lua API bindings for the `luna.stats` RPG character sheet system.  Exposes [`Sheet`] and a global [`StatsRegistry`] as `luna.stats.*` Lua values.

items.name: LuaSheet
items.description: Lua-visible wrapper for a character [`Sheet`].

items.name: register
items.description: Register the `luna.stats` module into the Lua state.

items.name: get_processor_count
items.description: Returns the number of logical processors available.  # Returns Logical CPU count; at least 1.

items.name: get_memory_size
items.description: Returns total system RAM in MiB using the `sysinfo` crate.  # Returns Physical memory in MiB (e.g. 16384 for 16 GB).

items.name: open_url
items.description: Opens a URL in the default browser/application.  Only `http://`, `https://`, and `mailto:` schemes are allowed.  # Parameters - `url` — the URL string to open  # Returns `true` if the command was spawned successfully; `false` if the scheme is rejected or the spawn failed.

items.name: get_preferred_locales
items.description: Returns the user's preferred locale strings.  # Returns A `Vec<String>` with at least one locale tag (e.g. `"en_US"`).

items.name: PowerState
items.description: Power state of the device.  # Variants - `Unknown` — cannot determine power state - `Battery` — running on battery - `NoBattery` — no battery (desktop plugged in) - `Charging` — battery is charging - `Charged` — battery is fully charged

items.name: get_power_info
items.description: Returns power/battery information: (state, percent, seconds).  On desktop platforms this returns `(Unknown, None, None)`.  # Returns A tuple of `(PowerState, Option<u32>, Option<u32>)` — the power state, remaining battery percentage (0–100), and remaining seconds on battery.

items.name: register
items.description: Registers `luna.system.*` platform query functions into the Lua VM.  # Parameters - `lua` — The active Lua VM instance. - `luna` — The `luna` global table to attach functions to.  # Returns `LuaResult<()>` — Ok if all functions were registered successfully; Lua error otherwise.

lua_api::thread_api.module_doc: Registers the `luna.thread` namespace.  Provides Lua-level multithreading: create background threads that run independent Lua VMs and communicate via thread-safe channels.

items.name: LuaThreadHandle
items.description: Lua UserData wrapper for a background thread handle.  # Fields - `inner` — `Arc<Mutex<LuaThread>>`.  Wraps `Arc<Mutex<LuaThread>>` so multiple Lua references can share access to the same thread state.

items.name: register
items.description: Registers all `luna.thread.*` functions into the Lua VM.  # Parameters - `lua` — `&Lua`. - `luna` — `&LuaTable`.  # Returns `LuaResult<()>`.  # Functions registered - `luna.thread.newThread(code)` — create a new background thread - `luna.thread.newChannel()` — create an unnamed channel - `luna.thread.getChannel(name)` — get or create a named global channel

lua_api::tilemap_api.module_doc: Registers the `luna.tilemap.*` tile map, tileset, autotile, and procedural generation API.  Exposes `LuaTileSet`, `LuaTileMap`, `LuaAutoTileSheet`, `LuaMapBlock`, `LuaMapGroup`, `LuaMapScript`, and `LuaMapGen` UserData types wrapping the `crate::tilemap` module.  All Lua-facing tile/layer/segment indices are **1-based**; Rust internals are 0-based.

items.name: LuaTileMap
items.description: Lua wrapper around a [`TileMap`].  # Fields - `inner` — `Rc<RefCell<TileMap>>`.

items.name: register
items.description: register.  # Parameters - `lua` — `&Lua`. - `luna` — `&LuaTable`.  # Returns `LuaResult<()>`.

items.name: register
items.description: Registers `luna.timer.*` functions into the Lua VM.  Provides frame-timing utilities: delta time, FPS, total time, average delta, high-precision timing, manual step control, and thread sleep.  # Parameters - `lua` — The active Lua VM instance. - `luna` — The `luna` global table to attach functions to. - `state` — Shared engine state accessed by the registered closures.  # Returns `LuaResult<()>` — Ok if all functions were registered successfully; Lua error otherwise.

items.name: register
items.description: Registers `luna.window.*` functions into the Lua VM.  # Parameters - `lua` — The active Lua VM instance. - `luna` — The `luna` global table to attach functions to. - `state` — Shared engine state accessed by the registered closures.  # Returns `LuaResult<()>` — Ok if all functions were registered successfully; Lua error otherwise.

math::bezier.module_doc: Bezier curve evaluation using De Casteljau's algorithm.  Supports arbitrary-degree curves with control point manipulation, rendering to polylines, derivative computation, and geometric transforms.

items.name: BezierCurve
items.description: A Bezier curve defined by control points.  Uses De Casteljau's algorithm for evaluation. Minimum 2 control points required.

math::easing.module_doc: Standard easing functions for smooth animation and interpolation.  Each function takes a normalised progress value `t` in `[0.0, 1.0]` and returns the eased value, also in `[0.0, 1.0]` for most curves.

items.name: linear
items.description: Linear interpolation — no easing.  # Parameters - `t` — normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_in_quad
items.description: Quadratic ease-in — starts slow, accelerates.  # Parameters - `t` — normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_out_quad
items.description: Quadratic ease-out — starts fast, decelerates.  # Parameters - `t` — normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_in_out_quad
items.description: Quadratic ease-in-out — slow start and end, fast middle.  # Parameters - `t` — normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_in_cubic
items.description: Cubic ease-in — starts slow, accelerates sharply.  # Parameters - `t` — normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_out_cubic
items.description: Cubic ease-out — starts fast, decelerates sharply.  # Parameters - `t` — normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_in_out_cubic
items.description: Cubic ease-in-out — smooth S-curve.  # Parameters - `t` — normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_in_quart
items.description: Quartic ease-in — very slow start.  # Parameters - `t` — normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_out_quart
items.description: Quartic ease-out — very slow end.  # Parameters - `t` — normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_in_out_quart
items.description: Quartic ease-in-out — pronounced S-curve.  # Parameters - `t` — normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_in_sine
items.description: Sinusoidal ease-in — gentle sine-based acceleration.  # Parameters - `t` — normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_out_sine
items.description: Sinusoidal ease-out — gentle sine-based deceleration.  # Parameters - `t` — normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_in_out_sine
items.description: Sinusoidal ease-in-out — gentle S-curve.  # Parameters - `t` — normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_in_expo
items.description: Exponential ease-in — very slow start, rapid acceleration.  # Parameters - `t` — normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_out_expo
items.description: Exponential ease-out — rapid start, very slow end.  # Parameters - `t` — normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_in_out_expo
items.description: Exponential ease-in-out — sharp S-curve with exponential tails.  # Parameters - `t` — normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_in_elastic
items.description: Elastic ease-in — spring-like overshoot at the start.  # Parameters - `t` — normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_out_elastic
items.description: Elastic ease-out — spring-like overshoot at the end.  # Parameters - `t` — normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_out_bounce
items.description: Bounce ease-out — simulates a bouncing ball landing.  # Parameters - `t` — normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_in_bounce
items.description: Bounce ease-in — simulates a bouncing ball launching.  # Parameters - `t` — normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_in_back
items.description: Back ease-in — pulls back before accelerating past the start.  # Parameters - `t` — normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_out_back
items.description: Back ease-out — overshoots the target then settles back.  # Parameters - `t` — normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: apply
items.description: Looks up an easing function by name and applies it to progress value `t`.  Supported names (case-insensitive): `"linear"`, `"inQuad"`, `"outQuad"`, `"inOutQuad"`, `"inCubic"`, `"outCubic"`, `"inOutCubic"`, `"inQuart"`, `"outQuart"`, `"inOutQuart"`, `"inSine"`, `"outSine"`, `"inOutSine"`, `"inExpo"`, `"outExpo"`, `"inOutExpo"`, `"inElastic"`, `"outElastic"`, `"outBounce"`, `"inBounce"`, `"inBack"`, `"outBack"`.  # Parameters - `name` — easing name string (case-insensitive) - `t` — normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns `Some(f32)` with the eased value, or `None` if the name is unrecognised.

math::geometry.module_doc: 2D geometry utility functions.  Free functions for common geometric computations: intersections, containment, polygon operations, and rasterization.

items.name: angle_between
items.description: Returns the angle in radians from (x1, y1) to (x2, y2).  # Parameters - `x1` — `f32`. - `y1` — `f32`. - `x2` — `f32`. - `y2` — `f32`.  # Returns `f32`.

items.name: circle_contains_point
items.description: Returns true if the point (px, py) is inside the circle centered at (cx, cy) with radius r.  # Parameters - `cx` — `f32`. - `cy` — `f32`. - `r` — `f32`. - `px` — `f32`. - `py` — `f32`.  # Returns `bool`.

items.name: circle_intersects_circle
items.description: Returns true if two circles overlap.  # Parameters - `x1` — `f32`. - `y1` — `f32`. - `r1` — `f32`. - `x2` — `f32`. - `y2` — `f32`. - `r2` — `f32`.  # Returns `bool`.

items.name: circle_intersects_line
items.description: Line-circle intersection. Returns (intersects, hit1, hit2).  # Parameters - `cx` — `f32`. - `cy` — `f32`. - `r` — `f32`. - `lx1` — `f32`. - `ly1` — `f32`. - `lx2` — `f32`. - `ly2` — `f32`.  # Returns `(bool, Option<(f32, f32)>, Option<(f32, f32)>)`.  Points are along the infinite line through (lx1,ly1)-(lx2,ly2).

items.name: circle_intersects_segment
items.description: Segment-circle intersection. Same as line-circle but clamped to the segment.  # Parameters - `cx` — `f32`. - `cy` — `f32`. - `r` — `f32`. - `sx1` — `f32`. - `sy1` — `f32`. - `sx2` — `f32`. - `sy2` — `f32`.  # Returns `(bool, Option<(f32, f32)>, Option<(f32, f32)>)`.

items.name: polygon_area
items.description: Computes the signed area of a polygon using the Shoelace formula.  # Parameters - `vertices` — `&[f32]`.  # Returns `f32`.  `vertices` is a flat array `[x0, y0, x1, y1, ...]`.

items.name: polygon_centroid
items.description: Computes the centroid of a polygon.  # Parameters - `vertices` — `&[f32]`.  # Returns `(f32, f32)`.  `vertices` is a flat array `[x0, y0, x1, y1, ...]`.

items.name: segment_intersects_segment
items.description: Tests if two line segments intersect. Returns (intersects, intersection_point).  # Parameters - `x1` — `f32`. - `y1` — `f32`. - `x2` — `f32`. - `y2` — `f32`. - `x3` — `f32`. - `y3` — `f32`. - `x4` — `f32`. - `y4` — `f32`.  # Returns `(bool, Option<(f32, f32)>)`.

items.name: closest_point_on_segment
items.description: Returns the closest point on a line segment to a given point.  # Parameters - `px` — `f32`. - `py` — `f32`. - `x1` — `f32`. - `y1` — `f32`. - `x2` — `f32`. - `y2` — `f32`.  # Returns `(f32, f32)`.

items.name: point_in_polygon
items.description: Tests if a point is inside a polygon using the ray casting algorithm.  # Parameters - `vertices` — `&[f32]`. - `px` — `f32`. - `py` — `f32`.  # Returns `bool`.  `vertices` is a flat array `[x0, y0, x1, y1, ...]`.

items.name: line_intersect
items.description: Infinite line intersection. Returns the intersection point if lines are not parallel.  # Parameters - `x1` — `f32`. - `y1` — `f32`. - `x2` — `f32`. - `y2` — `f32`. - `x3` — `f32`. - `y3` — `f32`. - `x4` — `f32`. - `y4` — `f32`.  # Returns `Option<(f32, f32)>`.

items.name: bresenham
items.description: Bresenham line rasterization from (x1, y1) to (x2, y2).  # Parameters - `x1` — `i32`. - `y1` — `i32`. - `x2` — `i32`. - `y2` — `i32`.  # Returns `Vec<(i32, i32)>`.  Returns all integer grid cells the line passes through.

items.name: convex_hull
items.description: Computes the convex hull of a set of 2D points using Andrew's monotone chain algorithm.  # Parameters - `points` — `&[f32]`.  # Returns `Vec<f32>`.  `points` is a flat array `[x0, y0, x1, y1, ...]`. Returns flat `[x0, y0, ...]`.

items.name: delaunay_triangulate
items.description: Delaunay triangulation using the Bowyer-Watson algorithm.  # Parameters - `points` — `&[(f64, f64)]`.  # Returns `Vec<[f64; 6]>`.  Returns a list of triangles, each as `[x1, y1, x2, y2, x3, y3]`.

math::grid.module_doc: 2D pathfinding grid with A*, Dijkstra, BFS, and flow field generation.  Cells are addressed with 0-based `(x, y)` coordinates. The Lua layer converts to/from 1-based indices.

items.name: Grid
items.description: 2D pathfinding grid with per-cell walkability and movement costs.  Supports A*, Dijkstra, and BFS pathfinding as well as flow field generation. All coordinates are 0-based.

items.name: Mat3
items.description: A 3×3 column-major matrix used for 2D affine transforms (translation, rotation, scale).  Used by `Camera::view_matrix` to combine position, rotation, and zoom into a single transform that maps world coordinates to screen coordinates.  # Fields - `m` — 3×3 array of `f32` in row-major layout (`m[row][col]`).

items.name: bezier
items.description: Bezier curve evaluation using De Casteljau's algorithm.

items.name: srgb
items.description: sRGB gamma ↔ linear color space conversion.

items.name: easing
items.description: Standard easing functions for smooth animation and interpolation.

items.name: geometry
items.description: 2D geometry utility functions: intersections, containment, polygon ops, rasterization.

items.name: grid
items.description: 2D pathfinding grid with A*, Dijkstra, BFS, and flow field generation.

items.name: mat3
items.description: 3x3 column-major matrix for 2D transforms (translate, rotate, scale).

items.name: noise
items.description: 2D Perlin and Simplex noise generators for procedural content.

items.name: polygon
items.description: Polygon utilities: ear-clipping triangulation and convexity testing.

items.name: procgen
items.description: Procedural generation: cellular automata, Voronoi, flood fill, Poisson disk, periodic noise.

items.name: random
items.description: Seedable random number generator for reproducible sequences.

items.name: raycasting
items.description: 2D raycasting and visibility utility functions.

items.name: rect
items.description: Axis-aligned rectangle with intersection and containment queries.

items.name: spatial_hash
items.description: Spatial hash for efficient broad-phase AABB collision queries.

items.name: transform
items.description: 2D affine transform with chainable methods wrapping Mat3.

items.name: tween
items.description: Value interpolator with easing curves.

items.name: vec2
items.description: 2D floating-point vector with arithmetic operators and common helpers.

math::noise.module_doc: 2D Perlin and Simplex noise generators for procedural content.  All functions return values in `[-1.0, 1.0]`. Seed is used to offset the gradient hash, producing different noise patterns per seed.

items.name: perlin2d
items.description: Generates 2D Perlin noise at the given coordinates.  # Parameters - `x` — X coordinate in noise space - `y` — Y coordinate in noise space - `seed` — numeric seed to vary the noise pattern  # Returns A value in approximately `[-1.0, 1.0]`.

items.name: simplex2d
items.description: Generates 2D Simplex noise at the given coordinates.  # Parameters - `x` — X coordinate in noise space - `y` — Y coordinate in noise space - `seed` — numeric seed to vary the noise pattern  # Returns A value in approximately `[-1.0, 1.0]`.

items.name: fbm
items.description: Generates fractal Brownian motion noise by layering multiple octaves of Perlin noise.  # Parameters - `x` — X coordinate in noise space - `y` — Y coordinate in noise space - `seed` — numeric seed - `octaves` — number of noise layers (1–8 typical) - `lacunarity` — frequency multiplier per octave (typical: 2.0) - `gain` — amplitude multiplier per octave (typical: 0.5)  # Returns A value centred around `0.0` in approximately `[-1.0, 1.0]`.

items.name: perlin3d
items.description: Generates 3D Perlin noise at the given coordinates.  # Parameters - `x` — X coordinate in noise space - `y` — Y coordinate in noise space - `z` — Z coordinate in noise space - `seed` — numeric seed to vary the noise pattern  # Returns A value in approximately `[-1.0, 1.0]`.

items.name: perlin4d
items.description: Generates 4D Perlin noise at the given coordinates.  # Parameters - `x` — X coordinate in noise space - `y` — Y coordinate in noise space - `z` — Z coordinate in noise space - `w_coord` — W coordinate in noise space - `seed` — numeric seed to vary the noise pattern  # Returns A value in approximately `[-1.0, 1.0]`.

items.name: DistType
items.description: Distance metric for Worley noise.  # Variants - `Euclidean` — Euclidean variant. - `Manhattan` — Manhattan variant. - `Chebyshev` — Chebyshev variant.

items.name: NoiseKind
items.description: Noise algorithm kind used by fractal combinators.  # Variants - `Perlin` — Perlin variant. - `Simplex` — Simplex variant.

items.name: FractalType
items.description: Fractal type for multi-octave noise.  # Variants - `Fbm` — Fbm variant. - `Ridged` — Ridged variant. - `Turbulence` — Turbulence variant.

items.name: MapGenOptions
items.description: Options for 2D noise map generation.  # Fields - `scale_x` — `f64`. - `scale_y` — `f64`. - `octaves` — `u32`. - `lacunarity` — `f64`. - `persistence` — `f64`. - `kind` — `NoiseKind`. - `fractal` — `FractalType`. - `offset_x` — `f64`. - `offset_y` — `f64`.

items.name: NoiseGenerator
items.description: Seeded procedural noise generator.  Holds a 512-entry permutation table derived deterministically from the seed.  All noise methods are pure functions of the seed and coordinates — the same inputs always produce the same output.

math::polygon.module_doc: Polygon utilities: ear-clipping triangulation and convexity testing.

items.name: triangulate
items.description: Triangulate a simple polygon using the ear-clipping algorithm.  # Parameters - `polygon` — slice of `Vec2` vertices forming a simple (non-self-intersecting) polygon;   must have at least 3 vertices  # Returns `Ok(triangles)` — a `Vec` of `[Vec2; 3]` triangle triples covering the polygon area. `Err(String)` — if triangulation fails (e.g. self-intersecting or degenerate input).

items.name: is_convex
items.description: Check if a polygon is convex.  Uses cross-product sign consistency at each vertex to determine convexity.  # Parameters - `polygon` — slice of `Vec2` vertices  # Returns `true` if the polygon is convex; `false` if concave, self-intersecting, or fewer than 3 vertices.

math::procgen.module_doc: Procedural generation utility functions.  Cave generation, Voronoi diagrams, flood fill, Poisson disk sampling, and periodic Perlin noise.

items.name: CellularOpts
items.description: Options for cellular automata generation.  # Fields - `fill` — `f32`. - `iterations` — `u32`. - `birth` — `u32`. - `survive` — `u32`. - `seed` — `u64`.

items.name: VoronoiOpts
items.description: Options for Voronoi diagram generation.  # Fields - `warp_scale` — `f32`. - `warp_strength` — `f32`. - `seed` — `u64`.

items.name: cellular_automata
items.description: Generates a cave/dungeon map using cellular automata.  # Parameters - `width` — `u32`. - `height` — `u32`. - `opts` — `&CellularOpts`.  # Returns `Vec<u8>`.  Returns a flat `Vec<u8>` of size `width * height` where 1 = wall, 0 = open.

items.name: voronoi_diagram
items.description: Generates a Voronoi diagram.  # Parameters - `width` — `u32`. - `height` — `u32`. - `points` — `&[(f32, f32)]`. - `opts` — `&VoronoiOpts`.  # Returns `(Vec<u32>, Vec<f32>, Vec<f32>)`.  Returns `(regions, distances, second_distances)`: - `regions`: flat `Vec<u32>` mapping each pixel to its closest point index - `distances`: distance to closest point - `second_distances`: distance to second-closest point

items.name: flood_fill
items.description: BFS flood fill on a grid.  # Parameters - `data` — `&[u8]`. - `width` — `u32`. - `height` — `u32`. - `sx` — `u32`. - `sy` — `u32`. - `threshold` — `u8`. - `above` — `bool`.  # Returns `Vec<u8>`.  - `data`: flat grid values - `threshold`: fill boundary value - `above`: if true, fill cells >= threshold; if false, fill cells <= threshold  Returns a `Vec<u8>` mask of same size (1 = filled, 0 = not).

items.name: poisson_disk
items.description: Generates Poisson disk sample points using Bridson's algorithm.  # Parameters - `width` — `f32`. - `height` — `f32`. - `min_dist` — `f32`. - `max_attempts` — `u32`. - `seed` — `u64`.  # Returns `Vec<(f32, f32)>`.  Returns a list of (x, y) points with minimum distance `min_dist` between them.

items.name: perlin_noise_periodic
items.description: Periodic Perlin noise that tiles over period (px, py).  # Parameters - `x` — `f64`. - `y` — `f64`. - `px` — `f64`. - `py` — `f64`.  # Returns `f64`.  Returns a value roughly in [-1, 1].

math::random.module_doc: Seedable random number generator for reproducible sequences.  Wraps `fastrand::Rng` with engine-compatible API including seeding, state save/restore, and normal distribution sampling.

items.name: RandomGenerator
items.description: Seedable random number generator exposed as a Lua object.  Wraps `fastrand::Rng` with engine-compatible API for deterministic random number generation, normal distribution, and state management.

math::raycasting.module_doc: 2D raycasting and visibility utility functions.  Provides ray-vs-segment intersection, field of view computation, column projection, and distance-based shading.

items.name: Segment
items.description: A line segment for raycasting.  # Fields - `x1` — `f32`. - `y1` — `f32`. - `x2` — `f32`. - `y2` — `f32`.

items.name: cast_ray_2d
items.description: Casts a ray from (ox, oy) in direction (dx, dy) against a list of segments.  # Parameters - `ox` — `f32`. - `oy` — `f32`. - `dx` — `f32`. - `dy` — `f32`. - `max_dist` — `f32`. - `segments` — `&[Segment]`.  # Returns `Option<(f32, f32, usize)>`.  Returns `Some((hit_x, hit_y, segment_index))` for the nearest hit within `max_dist`, or `None` if no segment is hit.

items.name: field_of_view
items.description: Computes a visibility polygon by casting rays at segment endpoints.  # Parameters - `ox` — `f32`. - `oy` — `f32`. - `segments` — `&[Segment]`. - `radius` — `f32`.  # Returns `Vec<f32>`.  Casts rays towards each segment endpoint (plus small angular offsets) within `radius`, sorts the hit points by angle, and returns a flat polygon `[x0, y0, x1, y1, ...]`.

items.name: project_column
items.description: Projects a wall column distance to screen-space drawing parameters.  # Parameters - `distance` — `f32`. - `fov` — `f32`. - `screen_height` — `f32`.  # Returns `(f32, f32, f32)`.  Returns (wall_height, draw_start, draw_end).

items.name: distance_shade
items.description: Distance-based shading. Returns brightness in [0, 1].  # Parameters - `distance` — `f32`. - `max_distance` — `f32`.  # Returns `f32`.  `(1 - distance / max_distance)` clamped to [0, 1].

items.name: RayHit
items.description: Result of a single ray cast.  # Fields - `distance` — `f32`. - `raw_distance` — `f32`. - `cell_value` — `u32`. - `side` — `u8`. - `tex_u` — `f32`. - `hit_x` — `f32`. - `hit_y` — `f32`. - `hit` — `bool`.

items.name: SpriteProjection
items.description: Sprite projection result.  # Fields - `screen_x` — `f32`. - `scale` — `f32`. - `distance` — `f32`. - `visible` — `bool`.

items.name: Raycaster2D
items.description: 2D grid-based raycaster using DDA traversal.  The grid stores wall types as `u32` values: 0 = empty, >0 = wall. Coordinates are 0-based with (0,0) at top-left.

items.name: Rect
items.description: An axis-aligned rectangle defined by its top-left corner and dimensions.  Used for AABB collision detection, UI layout, and camera viewport clipping.  # Fields - `x` — X coordinate of the top-left corner. - `y` — Y coordinate of the top-left corner. - `width` — Width in pixels or world units. - `height` — Height in pixels or world units.

math::spatial_hash.module_doc: Spatial hash for efficient broad-phase AABB collision queries.  Items are keyed by a string ID and stored with axis-aligned bounding boxes. The grid partitions space into fixed-size cells for O(1) bucket lookup.

items.name: SpatialItem
items.description: Entry in the spatial hash.  # Fields - `id` — `String`. - `x` — `f32`. - `y` — `f32`. - `w` — `f32`. - `h` — `f32`.

items.name: SpatialHash
items.description: Spatial hash for AABB queries.  Divides the 2D plane into a uniform grid of square cells. Each item is inserted into every cell its bounding box overlaps, enabling fast broad-phase collision queries.

math::srgb.module_doc: sRGB gamma ↔ linear color space conversion.  Implements the standard IEC 61966-2-1 (sRGB) transfer functions for converting between gamma-encoded sRGB and linear light values.

items.name: gamma_to_linear
items.description: Convert a single sRGB gamma-space color component to linear space.  Input and output in `[0.0, 1.0]`. Uses the standard IEC 61966-2-1 sRGB transfer function.  # Parameters - `c` — gamma-encoded sRGB channel value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Linear-light value in `[0.0, 1.0]`.

items.name: linear_to_gamma
items.description: Convert a single linear-space color component to sRGB gamma space.  Input and output in `[0.0, 1.0]`. Uses the standard IEC 61966-2-1 sRGB inverse transfer function.  # Parameters - `c` — linear-light channel value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Gamma-encoded sRGB value in `[0.0, 1.0]`.

math::transform.module_doc: 2D affine transform wrapping Mat3 with chainable methods.  Compatible with standard 2D transform API for translate, rotate, scale, shear, and point transformation.

items.name: Transform
items.description: 2D affine transform exposed as a Lua object.  Wraps `Mat3` with chainable transformation methods matching the standard 2D transform API.

math::tween.module_doc: Value interpolator with easing curves.  Uses easing functions from `crate::math::easing` to smoothly interpolate between start and target values over a duration.

items.name: TweenValue
items.description: A start-to-target value pair for interpolation.  # Fields - `start` — `f64`. - `target` — `f64`.

items.name: Tween
items.description: Value interpolator using easing functions.  Animates one or more values from start to target over a given duration, applying an easing curve to control the interpolation speed.

items.name: Vec2
items.description: A 2D floating-point vector used throughout the engine for positions, velocities, and directions.  Implements standard arithmetic operators (`+`, `-`, `*`, `/`, negation) and common geometric helpers. All operations are `Copy` — no references needed.  # Fields - `x` — Horizontal component. - `y` — Vertical component.

modding.module_doc: Mod management framework.  Provides `ModInfo` for mod metadata and `ModManager` for registration, dependency resolution, load ordering, folder scanning, and hot-reload queuing.

modding::mod_manager.module_doc: Mod management framework.  Provides `ModInfo` for mod metadata and `ModManager` for registration, dependency resolution, load ordering, folder scanning, and hot-reload queuing.

items.name: ModInfo
items.description: Metadata describing a mod.  # Fields - `id` — `String`. - `name` — `String`. - `version` — `String`. - `author` — `String`. - `description` — `String`. - `priority` — `i32`. - `dependencies` — `Vec<String>`. - `enabled` — `bool`. - `loaded` — `bool`. - `path` — `Option<String>`.

items.name: ModManager
items.description: Centralized registry for managing mods, resolving load order, validating dependencies, scanning mod folders, and queuing hot-reloads.

particle.module_doc: Particle system module providing emitter-based 2D particle effects.  A `ParticleSystem` spawns short-lived `Particle` entities each frame, advancing their position, velocity, and lifetime. Dead particles are recycled, keeping allocation bounded by `ParticleConfig::max_particles`.  Supports multi-stop size/color/alpha interpolation, emission shapes, radial/tangential acceleration, linear damping, relative mode, and texture-based rendering.

particle::system.module_doc: Particle system module providing emitter-based 2D particle effects.  A `ParticleSystem` spawns short-lived `Particle` entities each frame, advancing their position, velocity, and lifetime. Dead particles are recycled, keeping allocation bounded by `ParticleConfig::max_particles`.  Supports multi-stop size/color/alpha interpolation, emission shapes, radial/tangential acceleration, linear damping, relative mode, and texture-based rendering.

items.name: AreaDistribution
items.description: Area distribution mode for particle emission.  # Variants - `None` — None variant. - `Uniform` — Uniform variant. - `Normal` — Normal variant. - `Ellipse` — Ellipse variant. - `BorderEllipse` — BorderEllipse variant. - `BorderRectangle` — BorderRectangle variant.

items.name: InsertMode
items.description: Insert mode controlling where new particles are placed in the particle list.  # Variants - `Top` — Top variant. - `Bottom` — Bottom variant. - `Random` — Random variant.

items.name: EmitterState
items.description: Emitter lifecycle state.  # Variants - `Active` — Active variant. - `Paused` — Paused variant. - `Stopped` — Stopped variant.

items.name: EmissionShape
items.description: Emission shape controlling where new particles spawn relative to the emitter.  # Variants - `Point` — Point variant. - `Circle` — Circle variant. - `Rectangle` — Rectangle variant. - `Ring` — Ring variant. - `Line` — Line variant. - `Cone` — Cone variant. - `Star` — Star variant. - `Spiral` — Spiral variant.

items.name: RelativeMode
items.description: Relative mode controlling whether particles move with the emitter.  # Variants - `Detached` — Detached variant. - `Attached` — Attached variant.

items.name: ParticleConfig
items.description: Configuration for a particle emitter.  # Fields - `max_particles` — `u32`. - `emission_rate` — `f32`. - `lifetime_min` — `f32`. - `lifetime_max` — `f32`. - `speed_min` — `f32`. - `speed_max` — `f32`. - `direction` — `f32`. - `spread` — `f32`. - `gravity_x` — `f32`. - `gravity_y` — `f32`. - `sizes` — `Vec<f32>`. - `colors` — `Vec<[f32; 4]>`. - `spin_min` — `f32`. - `spin_max` — `f32`. - `rotation_min` — `f32`. - `rotation_max` — `f32`. - `spin_variation` — `f32`. - `size_variation` — `f32`. - `linear_accel_x_min` — `f32`. - `linear_accel_x_max` — `f32`. - `linear_accel_y_min` — `f32`. - `linear_accel_y_max` — `f32`. - `radial_accel_min` — `f32`. - `radial_accel_max` — `f32`. - `tangential_accel_min` — `f32`. - `tangential_accel_max` — `f32`. - `linear_damping_min` — `f32`. - `linear_damping_max` — `f32`. - `area_distribution` — `AreaDistribution`. - `area_width` — `f32`. - `area_height` — `f32`. - `area_angle` — `f32`. - `area_direction_relative` — `bool`. - `emitter_lifetime` — `f32`. - `insert_mode` — `InsertMode`. - `offset_x` — `f32`. - `offset_y` — `f32`. - `relative_rotation` — `bool`. - `texture_id` — `Option<TextureKey>`. - `quads` — `Vec<[f32; 4]>`. - `alpha_keyframes` — `Vec<f32>`. - `emission_shape` — `EmissionShape`. - `relative_mode` — `RelativeMode`. - `turbulence` — `f32`. - `drag` — `f32`. - `orbit_speed` — `f32`. - `animated_frames` — `u32`. - `frame_rate` — `f32`. - `color_by_speed` — `bool`. - `speed_color_min` — `f32`. - `speed_color_max` — `f32`.  All ranges are sampled uniformly per-particle at spawn time.

items.name: Particle
items.description: A single particle managed by a `ParticleSystem`.  # Fields - `x` — `f32`. - `y` — `f32`. - `vx` — `f32`. - `vy` — `f32`. - `life` — `f32`. - `max_life` — `f32`. - `rotation` — `f32`. - `spin` — `f32`. - `radial_accel` — `f32`. - `tangential_accel` — `f32`. - `linear_damping` — `f32`. - `size_variation` — `f32`. - `origin_x` — `f32`. - `origin_y` — `f32`.

items.name: ParticleSystem
items.description: An emitter-based particle system.  # Fields - `config` — `ParticleConfig`. - `particles` — `Vec<Particle>`. - `emitter_x` — `f32`. - `emitter_y` — `f32`. - `emit_accumulator` — `f32`. - `state` — `EmitterState`. - `emitter_age` — `f32`. - `prev_emitter_x` — `f32`. - `prev_emitter_y` — `f32`.  Call `update(dt)` each frame to advance physics and spawn new particles, then `draw_commands(ox, oy)` to obtain the `DrawCommand` list for rendering.

items.name: lerp
items.description: Linearly interpolate between `a` and `b` by factor `t`.  # Parameters - `a` — `f32`. - `b` — `f32`. - `t` — `f32`.  # Returns `f32`.

items.name: interpolate_sizes
items.description: Interpolate a multi-stop size array at normalised time `t` (0 = birth, 1 = death).  # Parameters - `sizes` — `&[f32]`. - `t` — `f32`. - `variation` — `f32`.  # Returns `f32`.  The `variation` factor (0..1) scales down the interpolated size. With `variation = 0`, the full interpolated size is returned.  # Edge cases - Empty `sizes`: returns `1.0`. - Single value: returns `sizes[0] * (1 - variation)`. - `t` is clamped to `[0, 1]`.

items.name: interpolate_colors
items.description: Interpolate a multi-stop color array at normalised time `t` (0 = birth, 1 = death).  # Parameters - `colors` — `&[[f32; 4]]`. - `t` — `f32`.  # Returns `[f32; 4]`.  # Edge cases - Empty `colors`: returns white `[1, 1, 1, 1]`. - Single value: returns that color. - `t` is clamped to `[0, 1]`.

items.name: interpolate_alphas
items.description: Interpolate a multi-stop alpha array at normalised time `t` (0 = birth, 1 = death).  # Parameters - `alphas` — `&[f32]`. - `t` — `f32`.  # Returns `f32`.  # Edge cases - Empty `alphas`: returns `1.0`. - Single value: returns `alphas[0]`. - `t` is clamped to `[0, 1]`.

pathfinding::astar.module_doc: A★ search with octile/Manhattan heuristic and Theta★ path smoothing.

items.name: astar
items.description: Run A★ search on `grid` from `start` to `goal`.  # Parameters - `grid` — `&NavGrid`. - `start` — `(u32, u32)`. - `goal` — `(u32, u32)`. - `unit_size` — `u32`. - `max_nodes` — `u32`.  # Returns `(Option<Vec<(u32, u32)>>, bool)`.  - `unit_size`: NxN footprint (1 for single-cell units). - `max_nodes`: upper bound on nodes expanded; 0 = unlimited.  Returns `(path, complete)` where `path` is `Some(vec)` with the path from start to goal (or nearest reachable node) and `complete` indicates whether the goal was actually reached.

items.name: line_of_sight
items.description: Check line-of-sight between two cells using Bresenham's algorithm,  # Parameters - `grid` — `&NavGrid`. - `x1` — `u32`. - `y1` — `u32`. - `x2` — `u32`. - `y2` — `u32`. - `unit_size` — `u32`.  # Returns `bool`. respecting `unit_size`×`unit_size` footprint.

items.name: smooth_path
items.description: Smooth a path by removing unnecessary waypoints via line-of-sight checks  # Parameters - `grid` — `&NavGrid`. - `path` — `&[(u32, u32)]`. - `unit_size` — `u32`.  # Returns `Vec<(u32, u32)>`. (Theta★-style post-processing).

pathfinding::async_pool.module_doc: Thread pool for asynchronous path computation.  Pathfinding requests are submitted with an ID and processed on worker threads. Results are collected via non-blocking [`PathThreadPool::poll`].

items.name: PathResult
items.description: A completed path result returned by [`PathThreadPool::poll`].

items.name: PathThreadPool
items.description: A pool of worker threads that process pathfinding requests asynchronously.

pathfinding::flow_field.module_doc: Flow field pathfinding for steering many units toward one or more targets.

items.name: FlowField
items.description: A pre-computed flow field that stores a direction vector and integrated cost for every cell, guiding any unit toward one or more target cells.

pathfinding::hpa.module_doc: Hierarchical Pathfinding A★ (HPA★) for fast long-range queries.  Divides the grid into chunks, finds boundary entrances, builds an abstract graph, and searches at the chunk level before refining to tile-level paths.

items.name: AbstractEdge
items.description: An edge in the abstract graph connecting two entrance nodes.  # Fields - `to` — `usize`. - `cost` — `f32`.

items.name: AbstractNode
items.description: A node in the abstract graph, representing an entrance point on a chunk boundary.  # Fields - `x` — `u32`. - `y` — `u32`. - `chunk` — `(u32, u32)`.

items.name: Chunk
items.description: A chunk region of the grid used during abstract graph construction.  # Fields - `col` — `u32`. - `row` — `u32`. - `x` — `u32`. - `y` — `u32`. - `w` — `u32`. - `h` — `u32`. - `entrance_indices` — `Vec<usize>`.

items.name: AbstractGraph
items.description: Pre-computed abstract graph for hierarchical A★ queries.  # Fields - `nodes` — `Vec<AbstractNode>`. - `edges` — `Vec<Vec<AbstractEdge>>`. - `chunks` — `HashMap<(u32, u32), Chunk>`. - `grid_width` — `u32`. - `grid_height` — `u32`. - `chunk_size` — `u32`.

items.name: build_abstract
items.description: Build the abstract graph from a `NavGrid`.  # Parameters - `grid` — `&NavGrid`. - `chunk_size` — `u32`.  # Returns `AbstractGraph`.  Divides the grid into `chunk_size × chunk_size` regions, finds entrance pairs on every inter-chunk boundary, and connects entrances within the same chunk via local A★.

items.name: hpa_star
items.description: Run HPA★ from `start` to `goal` on the abstract graph, then refine to tiles.  # Parameters - `grid` — `&NavGrid`. - `abstract_graph` — `&AbstractGraph`. - `start` — `(u32, u32)`. - `goal` — `(u32, u32)`. - `unit_size` — `u32`.  # Returns `Option<Vec<(u32, u32)>>`.  Returns `None` if no path exists. The returned path is tile-level.

items.name: is_reachable
items.description: Check if `goal` is reachable from `start` using the abstract graph.  # Parameters - `abstract_graph` — `&AbstractGraph`. - `start` — `(u32, u32)`. - `goal` — `(u32, u32)`.  # Returns `bool`.  This is a fast connectivity check — it doesn't compute the actual path.

pathfinding.module_doc: Grid-based pathfinding: A★, HPA★, flow fields, and unit-size-aware navigation.

items.name: astar
items.description: astar.

items.name: async_pool
items.description: async_pool.

items.name: flow_field
items.description: flow_field.

items.name: hpa
items.description: hpa.

items.name: nav_grid
items.description: nav_grid.

items.name: unit_pathfinder
items.description: unit_pathfinder.

pathfinding::nav_grid.module_doc: Navigation grid with per-cell traversal costs and diagonal movement modes.

items.name: DiagonalMode
items.description: Controls how diagonal movement is handled during pathfinding.  # Variants - `None` — None variant. - `Always` — Always variant. - `NoCornerCut` — NoCornerCut variant.

items.name: NavGrid
items.description: A 2D grid of traversal costs used by pathfinding algorithms.  Cells are addressed with 0-based `(x, y)` coordinates in row-major order. A cost of `0` marks a cell as blocked; values `1..=255` represent movement cost.

pathfinding::unit_pathfinder.module_doc: Unit-aware pathfinder with result caching and convenience methods.

items.name: Waypoint
items.description: A waypoint along a computed path.  # Fields - `x` — `u32`. - `y` — `u32`.

items.name: UnitPathfinder
items.description: A pathfinder that operates on a shared `NavGrid` with optional result caching.

items.name: BodyType
items.description: Determines whether a physics body is affected by forces and gravity.  # Variants - `Static` — Immovable body; does not respond to gravity or impulses. Used for terrain. - `Dynamic` — Movable body; subject to gravity, forces, and velocity integration. - `Sensor` — Trigger volume; detects overlaps but does not resolve collisions.

items.name: BodyShape
items.description: Describes the collision geometry of a body.  # Variants - `Rect { width, height }` — Axis-aligned rectangle (AABB). Default shape. - `Circle { radius }` — Circle; uses distance-based collision detection.

items.name: Body
items.description: A rigid body with position, velocity, mass, shape, and restitution.  Bodies live in a `World` and are identified by their index. Use `World::get_body_mut` to access and modify them each frame.  # Fields - `position` — World-space centre position. - `velocity` — Linear velocity in world units per second. - `mass` — Mass in kg. - `body_type` — Static, Dynamic, Kinematic, or Sensor. - `shape` — Collision geometry (Rect or Circle). - `restitution` — Bounciness coefficient. - `layer` / `mask` — Collision filter bitmasks. - `friction` — Friction coefficient. - `angle` — Angular orientation in radians. - `angular_velocity` — Spin rate in radians/s. - `shape_ext` — Extended shape override (polygon, edge, chain).

items.name: CollisionInfo
items.description: Collision contact data: penetration depth and separating normal.  Retained for backward compatibility; collision detection is now handled by rapier2d internally. See `World::get_collision_events()` for events.  # Fields - `penetration` — How much the two bodies overlap along the minimum separation axis. - `normal` — Unit vector pointing from body B towards body A.

physics.module_doc: Physics simulation module backed by rapier2d.  Provides rigid-body simulation with circles, rectangles, sensors, raycasting, joints, and collision event recording.

items.name: body
items.description: Body types, shapes, and the `Body` struct used by the physics world.

items.name: collision
items.description: Collision event structs returned by `World::get_collision_events`.

items.name: shape
items.description: Extended shape types: Polygon, Edge, and Chain colliders.

items.name: world
items.description: The `World` struct and all simulation management: bodies, joints, raycasting, contacts.

physics::shape.module_doc: Extended shape types for physics bodies.  Provides polygon, edge, and chain shapes beyond the basic rect/circle.

items.name: Shape
items.description: Extended collision shape for physics bodies.  Goes beyond `BodyShape` to support convex polygons, edges, and chains.  # Variants - `Rect` — Axis-aligned rectangle with width and height. - `Circle` — Circle with a radius. - `Polygon` — Convex polygon (max 8 vertices). - `Edge` — Line segment between two points. - `Chain` — Connected chain of edges, optionally closed.

physics::world.module_doc: Rapier2d-backed physics world with backward-compatible Luna2D API.  Wraps the rapier2d simulation pipeline while exposing the same `Body`-based interface. The `Body` struct is a sync buffer: mutations via `get_body_mut()` are flushed into rapier on the next `step()` call, and rapier's computed positions/velocities are read back after each step.

items.name: BodyContact
items.description: Collision event generated by `World::step` when two bodies start overlapping.  Contains stable integer body IDs for the two bodies involved.  # Fields - `body_a` — Index of the first body. - `body_b` — Index of the second body.

items.name: RaycastHit
items.description: Result of a `World::raycast` query.  # Fields - `body_id` — Integer body ID that was hit. - `point` — World-space hit point `(x, y)`. - `normal` — World-space surface normal `(nx, ny)`. - `toi` — Distance along the ray to the hit point.

items.name: ContactInfo
items.description: Contact information between two bodies from the narrow phase.  # Fields - `body_a` — Index of the first body. - `body_b` — Index of the second body. - `normal_x` — Contact normal X component. - `normal_y` — Contact normal Y component. - `is_touching` — Whether the bodies are currently touching.

items.name: World
items.description: Simulates a 2D physics world using rapier2d.  Bodies are stored as a `Vec<Body>` for backward-API compatibility. Each `step()` syncs mutations into rapier, runs the pipeline, then reads back positions/velocities for Dynamic bodies.

quest.module_doc: Quest and objective tracking system for Luna2D games.  Provides `Quest`, `Objective`, `QuestStage`, `JournalEntry`, and `QuestLog` for building RPG-style quest systems with stages, conditions, and journal notes.

items.name: QuestStatus
items.description: Lifecycle state of a quest.

items.name: ObjectiveStatus
items.description: Lifecycle state of a single objective.

items.name: Objective
items.description: A single trackable task within a quest.  Supports count-based progress (`current`/`required`) and optional tagging.

items.name: QuestStage
items.description: A named group of objectives that represent one phase of a quest.

items.name: JournalEntry
items.description: A timestamped text entry in a quest's journal.

items.name: Quest
items.description: A quest with stages, objectives, and a journal.  Stages are ordered; the quest advances through them as the game logic calls `next_stage()` or `goto_stage()`.

items.name: QuestLog
items.description: Registry of all quests for a game session.  Quests are stored by their id. Active/completed/available sets are derived by filtering on status rather than maintaining parallel lists.

resource.module_doc: Resource economy system: named resources with capacity, flow rates, decay, interest, upkeep, overflow policies, reservations, and conversions.  Designed for RTS, management, survival, and RPG economy patterns where the game tracks multiple named numeric values (gold, wood, mana, food) that change over time with configurable rates.

items.name: OverflowPolicy
items.description: Policy governing what happens when adding exceeds capacity.

items.name: Resource
items.description: A single named numeric resource with rates, capacity, overflow policy, and reservation.

items.name: ModifierType
items.description: Modifier type for conversion rules.

items.name: Modifier
items.description: A rate modifier that can be attached to conversion rules.

items.name: ConversionRule
items.description: A rule for converting one resource type to another.

items.name: ResourceManager
items.description: A manager that owns named resources and provides bulk operations and conversions.

savegame.module_doc: Save/load slot system with collectors, schema versioning, dirty tracking, and auto-save.

savegame::save_data.module_doc: Save/load slot system with collectors, schema versioning, dirty tracking, and auto-save.

items.name: SlotMeta
items.description: Metadata extracted from a save slot.  # Fields - `slot` — `String`. - `timestamp` — `f64`. - `version` — `i32`. - `summary` — `String`.

items.name: SaveManager
items.description: Pure-data save manager providing registration of named collectors, schema versioning, dirty-state tracking, and auto-save timer.  Actual serialisation and filesystem calls happen on the Lua side; this struct tracks the bookkeeping.

items.name: serialize_table
items.description: Serialize a simple Lua-compatible value hierarchy into a `return { ... }` string.  # Parameters - `data` — `&HashMap<String, SaveValue>`. - `depth` — `u32`.  # Returns `Result<String, String>`.  Supports nil, bool, number (f64), string, and nested tables (HashMap). Does not handle userdata, functions, or circular references.

items.name: serialize_value
items.description: Serialize a single value.  # Parameters - `value` — `&SaveValue`. - `depth` — `u32`.  # Returns `Result<String, String>`.

items.name: SaveValue
items.description: A simple value type matching the Lua subset we can serialize.  # Variants - `Nil` — Nil variant. - `Bool` — Bool variant. - `Number` — Number variant. - `Str` — Str variant. - `Table` — Table variant.

items.name: DepthEntry
items.description: Entry in the depth-sorted draw queue.  # Fields - `depth` — `f32`. - `callback_index` — `usize`. - `is_object` — `bool`.

items.name: DepthSorter
items.description: Per-frame depth-sorted draw batcher.  Collects draw callbacks with depth values, sorts them, and flushes them in ascending depth order each frame.

scene.module_doc: Scene stack module for managing game scene lifecycle, transitions, and depth-sorted rendering.  Provides a LIFO stack of Lua scene tables with lifecycle callbacks (`enter`, `leave`, `pause`, `resume`, `update`, `draw`), animated transitions (fade, slide), a named scene registry, inter-scene data passing, and a `DepthSorter` for z-ordered draw batching.

items.name: depth_sorter
items.description: depth_sorter.

items.name: stack
items.description: stack.

items.name: transition
items.description: transition.

scene::stack.module_doc: LIFO scene stack with registry and inter-scene data store.

items.name: SceneId
items.description: Unique identifier for a scene in the stack.

items.name: SceneStack
items.description: The scene stack manages a LIFO stack of scene references.  Scenes are identified by `SceneId` values. The Lua API layer maps these to `mlua::RegistryKey` references for actual Lua table access.

scene::transition.module_doc: Visual transition types and active transition state for scene changes.

items.name: TransitionType
items.description: Visual transition types between scenes.  # Variants - `None` — None variant. - `Fade` — Fade variant. - `SlideLeft` — SlideLeft variant. - `SlideRight` — SlideRight variant. - `SlideUp` — SlideUp variant. - `SlideDown` — SlideDown variant.

items.name: ActiveTransition
items.description: Active transition state tracking progress between two scenes.  # Fields - `transition_type` — `TransitionType`. - `duration` — `f32`. - `elapsed` — `f32`.

sound::midi.module_doc: MIDI SoundFont state management.  Provides `MidiState` for tracking whether a SoundFont (SF2) file has been loaded for MIDI instrument rendering.

items.name: MidiState
items.description: MIDI SoundFont state.  Tracks whether a SoundFont (SF2) file has been loaded and stores its raw bytes for future MIDI decoding.

sound.module_doc: Decoded audio sample manipulation and MIDI SoundFont state.  Provides `SoundData` for reading and writing decoded PCM audio samples, and `MidiState` for managing MIDI SoundFont (SF2) loading.

items.name: sound_data
items.description: Decoded PCM sample buffer with per-sample read/write access.

items.name: midi
items.description: MIDI SoundFont state management.

items.name: SoundData
items.description: Decoded audio samples in f32 PCM format.  Stores interleaved samples (for stereo: L, R, L, R, ...).

stats.module_doc: RPG character sheet and stat system.  Provides a flexible attribute system with buffs, derived stats, traits, skills, perks, XP/levelling, action points, morale, and resistances.

items.name: StackMode
items.description: How a buff stacks with existing buffs of the same name.

items.name: Buff
items.description: A single stat modifier attached to an attribute.

items.name: Attribute
items.description: A named stat attribute with base value, constraints, and regen.

items.name: Skill
items.description: A named skill with cooldown, resource cost, and level tracking.

items.name: Perk
items.description: A named perk requiring a minimum level to acquire.

items.name: TraitDef
items.description: A named trait definition (a bundle of buff descriptors).

items.name: ActionPoints
items.description: Action point tracking for turn-based games.

items.name: Morale
items.description: Morale state with panic/berserk thresholds.

items.name: LevelThresholds
items.description: Level threshold setting: either a static table or a formula.

items.name: Sheet
items.description: The central character sheet, holding all stat data.

items.name: StatsRegistry
items.description: Global registry for traits and archetypes. Separate from Sheet for sharing.

thread::channel.module_doc: Thread-safe channel for Lua inter-thread communication.  Provides an MPMC (multi-producer, multi-consumer) queue that can be shared between the main game thread and background Lua threads via `Arc<Channel>`. Only primitive Lua values (nil, bool, number, string) can cross thread boundaries.

items.name: ChannelValue
items.description: Serializable values that can be sent between threads.  # Variants - `Nil` — Nil variant. - `Bool` — Bool variant. - `Number` — Number variant. - `String` — String variant.  Only Lua-native primitive types are supported; UserData, tables, and functions cannot cross thread boundaries.

items.name: Channel
items.description: Thread-safe MPMC channel for Lua inter-thread communication.  Internally uses a `Mutex<VecDeque>` protected queue with a `Condvar` for blocking `demand()` calls. Safe to wrap in `Arc` and share across OS threads.

items.name: LuaChannel
items.description: Lua UserData wrapper for a thread-safe channel.  # Fields - `inner` — `Arc<Channel>`.  Holds an `Arc<Channel>` so the underlying channel can be shared across threads while each Lua VM holds its own `LuaChannel` handle.

items.name: lua_to_channel_value
items.description: Convert a Lua value into a `ChannelValue` for cross-thread transfer.  # Parameters - `value` — `LuaValue`.  # Returns `LuaResult<ChannelValue>`.  Only nil, boolean, number (integer or float), and string are supported. Returns a Lua runtime error for unsupported types.

items.name: channel_value_to_lua
items.description: Convert a `ChannelValue` back into a Lua value.  # Parameters - `lua` — `&Lua`. - `value` — `ChannelValue`.  # Returns `LuaResult<LuaValue<'_>>`.

thread.module_doc: Thread infrastructure: inter-thread channel and background Lua worker.

thread::worker.module_doc: Background Lua thread with independent VM.  Each `LuaThread` spawns an OS thread running its own `mlua::Lua` instance. Communication with the main thread happens exclusively through `Channel` objects — no Lua state is shared across threads.

items.name: ThreadState
items.description: Execution state of a background Lua thread.  # Variants - `Pending` — Pending variant. - `Running` — Running variant. - `Completed` — Completed variant. - `Error` — Error variant.

items.name: LuaThread
items.description: A background Lua thread running its own VM.  Created via `luna.thread.newThread(code)`. Call `start()` to spawn the OS thread and `wait()` to block until completion. Errors are captured in `ThreadState::Error` and retrievable via `get_error()`.

tilemap::autotile_sheet.module_doc: AutoTileSheet — loads a pre-arranged autotile image and generates TileSet tiles + bitmask rules.  ## 48-tile quarter-tile format  For high-quality composite autotile rendering, [`AutoTileSheet`] supports a 48-tile sub-tile format where each full tile is assembled from **four quarter-tile pieces** (TL, TR, BL, BR).  The source image must be **`3*tile_w` × `4*tile_h` pixels** — a 6-column × 8-row grid of `(tile_w/2 × tile_h/2)` quarter pieces.  Call [`AutoTileSheet::get_quarter_rects`] with the raw 8-bit neighbour bitmask to obtain the four [`Rect`]s to blit.  Use [`AutoTileSheet::get_composite48_grid_quad`] when the source atlas stores the 48 pre-composed tile variants in a 6 × 8 full-tile grid instead.  ### Bitmask bit layout (raw 8-bit)  ```text bit 0 = N   bit 4 = NE bit 1 = E   bit 5 = SE bit 2 = S   bit 6 = SW bit 3 = W   bit 7 = NW ```

items.name: AutoTileLayout
items.description: Predefined autotile sheet layout variants.  # Variants - `Blob47` — Blob47 variant. - `Composite48` — 48-tile composite variant. - `Minimal16` — Minimal16 variant.

items.name: AutoTileSheet
items.description: An autotile sheet that maps layout conventions to bitmask-based tile selection.  Stores the bitmask↔tile-index mapping tables for a given [`AutoTileLayout`] and can apply the resulting rules to a [`TileSet`].

tilemap::chunk.module_doc: Chunk-based tilemap storage for large and infinite maps.  [`ChunkMap`] stores tiles in fixed-size square chunks using a [`HashMap`]. Only chunks that have been written to are allocated, allowing sparse, large, or infinite maps without up-front memory commitment.  Tile coordinates use `i32` to support negative positions (e.g., a map centred at the origin where negative X/Y are valid map space).

items.name: ChunkMap
items.description: A chunk-based tilemap that supports large and infinite maps through sparse storage.  Tiles are stored in square chunks of `chunk_size × chunk_size` tiles.  Chunks are allocated on first write and can be individually unloaded to reclaim memory. Reads from unloaded chunks return [`ChunkMap::DEFAULT_GID`] (0).

tilemap::coords.module_doc: Coordinate conversion functions for isometric and hexagonal tile grids.

items.name: to_screen_iso
items.description: Converts tile coordinates to screen position using diamond isometric projection.  # Parameters - `tx` — `f32`. - `ty` — `f32`. - `tile_w` — `f32`. - `tile_h` — `f32`.  # Returns `Vec2`.  - `screen.x = (tx - ty) * tile_w / 2` - `screen.y = (tx + ty) * tile_h / 2`

items.name: from_screen_iso
items.description: Converts screen position back to tile coordinates for diamond isometric projection.  # Parameters - `sx` — `f32`. - `sy` — `f32`. - `tile_w` — `f32`. - `tile_h` — `f32`.  # Returns `Vec2`.

items.name: iso_rotate
items.description: Rotates an isometric direction (1–4) clockwise by `steps`.  # Parameters - `direction` — `i32`. - `steps` — `i32`.  # Returns `i32`.  Directions: 1=south, 2=west, 3=north, 4=east.

items.name: iso_direction_name
items.description: Returns the name of an isometric direction (1–4).  # Parameters - `direction` — `i32`.  # Returns `&'static str`.  Returns `"unknown"` for out-of-range values.

items.name: iso_direction_from_angle
items.description: Snaps an angle (in radians) to the nearest isometric direction (1–4).  # Parameters - `angle` — `f32`.  # Returns `i32`.  - south (down, π/2) → 1 - west (left, π) → 2 - north (up, -π/2) → 3 - east (right, 0) → 4

items.name: to_screen_hex
items.description: Converts axial hex coordinates to screen position (pointy-top layout).  # Parameters - `q` — `i32`. - `r` — `i32`. - `size` — `f32`.  # Returns `Vec2`.  - `x = size * √3 * (q + r/2)` - `y = size * 3/2 * r`

items.name: from_screen_hex
items.description: Converts screen position back to axial hex coordinates (pointy-top layout).  # Parameters - `sx` — `f32`. - `sy` — `f32`. - `size` — `f32`.  # Returns `(i32, i32)`.  Uses fractional axial reverse, then rounds with [`hex_round`].

items.name: hex_neighbors
items.description: Returns the six axial neighbor offsets for pointy-top hexagonal grids.  # Parameters - `q` — `i32`. - `r` — `i32`.  # Returns `[(i32, i32); 6]`.

items.name: hex_distance
items.description: Returns the hex distance between two axial coordinates using cube distance.  # Parameters - `q1` — `i32`. - `r1` — `i32`. - `q2` — `i32`. - `r2` — `i32`.  # Returns `i32`.

items.name: hex_round
items.description: Rounds fractional axial coordinates to the nearest hex cell using cube rounding.  # Parameters - `q` — `f32`. - `r` — `f32`.  # Returns `(i32, i32)`.

items.name: hex_line
items.description: Returns all hex cells along a line between two axial coordinates.  # Parameters - `q1` — `i32`. - `r1` — `i32`. - `q2` — `i32`. - `r2` — `i32`.  # Returns `Vec<(i32, i32)>`.  Uses linear interpolation in cube space with [`hex_round`] at each step.

items.name: hex_ring
items.description: Returns all cells at exactly `radius` distance from `(q, r)`.  # Parameters - `q` — `i32`. - `r` — `i32`. - `radius` — `i32`.  # Returns `Vec<(i32, i32)>`.  For radius 0, returns just the center cell.

items.name: hex_spiral
items.description: Returns all hex cells from center outward to `radius`, ring by ring.  # Parameters - `q` — `i32`. - `r` — `i32`. - `radius` — `i32`.  # Returns `Vec<(i32, i32)>`.

items.name: hex_area
items.description: Returns all hex cells within `radius` distance (filled hex circle).  # Parameters - `q` — `i32`. - `r` — `i32`. - `radius` — `i32`.  # Returns `Vec<(i32, i32)>`.

items.name: hex_rotate
items.description: Rotates hex coordinates `(q, r)` around `(center_q, center_r)` by `steps × 60°` clockwise.  # Parameters - `q` — `i32`. - `r` — `i32`. - `center_q` — `i32`. - `center_r` — `i32`. - `steps` — `i32`.  # Returns `(i32, i32)`.  Uses cube coordinate rotation.

items.name: hex_reflect
items.description: Reflects hex coordinates across an axis through the center.  # Parameters - `q` — `i32`. - `r` — `i32`. - `center_q` — `i32`. - `center_r` — `i32`. - `axis` — `&str`.  # Returns `(i32, i32)`.  Axis must be `"q"`, `"r"`, or `"s"`. Uses cube coordinate reflection.

tilemap::isomap.module_doc: Multi-level isometric tilemap for OpenXcom-style rendering.  Each map cell holds four tile parts rendered in painter's algorithm order: `Floor`, `NorthWall`, `WestWall`, `Object`. Z-levels stack vertically with a configurable pixel offset so higher floors appear above lower ones.

items.name: IsoTilePart
items.description: The four sub-slots within each isometric map cell, rendered in this order.  # Variants - `Floor` — Floor variant. - `NorthWall` — NorthWall variant. - `WestWall` — WestWall variant. - `Object` — Object variant.  Matching OpenXcom's FLOOR → NORTH_WALL → WEST_WALL → OBJECT sequence ensures correct painter's algorithm occlusion within a single cell.

items.name: IsoTile
items.description: One map cell containing four GIDs, one per [`IsoTilePart`].  # Fields - `parts` — `[u32; 4]`.  A GID of `0` means the slot is empty — `draw_iter` still yields the item so callers can decide whether to skip it.

items.name: IsoLevel
items.description: One Z-level of the isometric map — a 2-D grid of [`IsoTile`]s.  # Fields - `width` — `u32`. - `height` — `u32`. - `visible` — `bool`.

items.name: IsoDrawItem
items.description: One renderable item produced by [`IsoMap::draw_iter`] in painter's order.  # Fields - `level` — `u32`. - `tile_x` — `u32`. - `tile_y` — `u32`. - `part` — `u32`. - `gid` — `u32`. - `screen_x` — `f32`. - `screen_y` — `f32`.  `level`, `tile_x`, `tile_y`, and `part` are all **0-based** Rust values. The Lua API converts them to 1-based before passing them to scripts.

items.name: IsoMap
items.description: Multi-level isometric tilemap with painter's-algorithm draw iteration.  # Fields - `width` — `u32`. - `height` — `u32`. - `tile_w` — `u32`. - `tile_h` — `u32`. - `level_height` — `u32`. - `origin_x` — `f32`. - `origin_y` — `f32`.  # Coordinate system  ```text screen_x = origin_x + (tx - ty) * tile_w / 2 screen_y = origin_y + (tx + ty) * tile_h / 2 - tz * level_height ```  # Draw order  [`draw_iter`](IsoMap::draw_iter) yields items in the correct painter's algorithm order for a standard isometric view with the camera positioned at the top-left corner:  1. Diagonal `d = tx + ty` increases from `0` to `(W-1) + (H-1)`. 2. For each diagonal, tiles iterate in ascending `tx` order. 3. For each tile, Z-levels iterate from bottom (`0`) to `active_z`. 4. For each Z-level, parts iterate Floor → NorthWall → WestWall → Object.

tilemap::mapgen.module_doc: Procedural map generation — MapBlock, MapGroup, MapScript, MapGen.

items.name: Edge
items.description: Cardinal edge direction for block-segment connectivity.  # Variants - `North` — North variant. - `East` — East variant. - `South` — South variant. - `West` — West variant.

items.name: MapBlock
items.description: A prefab grid of tiles that can be stamped into a generated map.  Contains multi-layer tile data and per-segment edge connection IDs.

items.name: MapGroup
items.description: A biome-like container holding [`MapBlock`] prefabs and [`MapScript`] generators.

items.name: StepType
items.description: The type of operation a [`ScriptStep`] performs.  # Variants - `FillRandom` — FillRandom variant. - `PlaceBlock` — PlaceBlock variant. - `PlaceRandom` — PlaceRandom variant. - `PlaceLine` — PlaceLine variant. - `FloodFill` — FloodFill variant. - `FillArea` — FillArea variant. - `DrawPath` — DrawPath variant. - `FillRect` — FillRect variant.

items.name: ScriptStep
items.description: A single step in a [`MapScript`] with rich configuration.  # Fields - `step_type` — `StepType`. - `group_index` — `i32`. - `block_index` — `i32`. - `x` — `u32`. - `y` — `u32`. - `width` — `u32`. - `height` — `u32`. - `count` — `u32`. - `rotation` — `u32`. - `mirror` — `bool`. - `random_rotation` — `bool`. - `random_mirror` — `bool`. - `direction` — `u32`. - `match_sides` — `bool`. - `condition_step` — `i32`. - `condition_success` — `bool`. - `chance` — `f32`. - `repeat_count` — `u32`. - `min_count` — `i32`. - `max_count` — `i32`. - `size_filter_w` — `i32`. - `size_filter_h` — `i32`. - `tile_id` — `u32`. - `path_width` — `u32`. - `tile_layer` — `u32`. - `zone_start_y` — `i32`. - `zone_end_y` — `i32`.

items.name: MapScript
items.description: A named sequence of [`ScriptStep`]s that drives procedural generation.

items.name: MapOrientation
items.description: Map orientation for visual layout hints.  # Variants - `TopDown` — TopDown variant. - `SideView` — SideView variant.

items.name: LayerMode
items.description: How layers are managed during generation.  # Variants - `Unified` — Unified variant. - `Independent` — Independent variant.

items.name: MapSize
items.description: Predefined map size presets expressed in segment-grid units.  # Variants - `Small` — Small variant. - `Medium` — Medium variant. - `Large` — Large variant. - `Custom` — Custom variant.

items.name: MapZone
items.description: A named horizontal zone within a generated map.  # Fields - `name` — `String`. - `start_row` — `u32`. - `height` — `u32`.

items.name: MapGen
items.description: Top-level procedural map generator.  Produces a [`TileMap`] by stamping [`MapBlock`] prefabs according to [`MapScript`] steps, using deterministic randomness.

tilemap.module_doc: Tilemap engine module — TileSet, TileMap, AutoTileSheet, IsoMap, ChunkMap, TMX loader, and procedural generation types.

items.name: autotile_sheet
items.description: Auto-tile atlas with bitmask-based tile selection.

items.name: chunk
items.description: Sparse chunk-based tilemap storage for large and infinite maps.

items.name: coords
items.description: Coordinate helpers for tile ↔ world conversions.

items.name: isomap
items.description: Multi-level isometric tilemap with painter's-algorithm draw order.

items.name: mapgen
items.description: Procedural map generation scripts and room placement.

items.name: tilemap
items.description: Core tilemap: layers, tile CRUD, sweep queries, and serialization.

items.name: tileset
items.description: Tile set definition with per-tile properties and animation frames.

items.name: tmx
items.description: Tiled TMX/TSX map format parser.

items.name: tile_walker
items.description: Grid-direction walker for tile stepping.

tilemap::tile_walker.module_doc: Tile-based first-person movement controller with cardinal directions.

items.name: Facing
items.description: Cardinal facing direction.  # Variants - `North` — North variant. - `East` — East variant. - `South` — South variant. - `West` — West variant.

items.name: TileWalker
items.description: Tile-based movement controller for first-person grid navigation.  Operates on integer grid coordinates with cardinal facing directions. Optionally references a `Raycaster2D` for collision checking.

tilemap::tilemap.module_doc: Main tile map container with layers, tiles, viewport, collision, and autotile support.

items.name: TileLayer
items.description: A single layer of tiles in a [`TileMap`].  # Fields - `name` — `String`. - `width` — `u32`. - `height` — `u32`. - `visible` — `bool`. - `tint` — `[f32; 4]`. - `offset` — `Vec2`. - `parallax` — `Vec2`.  Tiles are stored in row-major order. GID 0 means "empty / no tile".

items.name: SweepResult
items.description: Result of a swept-AABB collision test against solid tiles.  # Fields - `contact_point` — `Vec2`. - `normal` — `Vec2`. - `tile_x` — `u32`. - `tile_y` — `u32`. - `t` — `f32`.

items.name: TileMap
items.description: A 2D tile map composed of layers, tilesets, and viewport-clipped rendering state.  All tile coordinates are **0-based**. Lua helpers add 1-based conversion externally.

tilemap::tileset.module_doc: Tile set backed by a texture atlas with animation, solid flags, and autotile rules.

items.name: TileAnimFrame
items.description: A single frame in a tile animation sequence.  # Fields - `tile_id` — `u32`. - `duration_ms` — `f32`.

items.name: TileSet
items.description: A tile set that maps local tile IDs to atlas regions, animations, and collision flags.  Tile IDs are 0-based within the set. The `first_gid` maps this set into the global GID space used by [`super::TileMap`].

tilemap::tmx.module_doc: Tiled TMX map format loader.  Parses `.tmx` files produced by the [Tiled Map Editor](https://www.mapeditor.org/). TMX is an XML-based format; this module uses [`roxmltree`] for parsing.  Supports: - Orthogonal, isometric, staggered, and hexagonal map orientations. - Tile data encoding: XML elements, CSV, and Base64 (with optional zlib/gzip compression). - Inline and external tileset references (TSX source returned as a path string for caller resolution). - Object layers and image layers (parsed to metadata; not converted to [`TileMap`] data).  Does NOT support: - Infinite maps (chunk-based TMX format): treat as a warning; use [`ChunkMap`] directly. - Embedded TSX tileset files — the caller must load them separately.  [`ChunkMap`]: super::chunk::ChunkMap

items.name: TmxOrientation
items.description: Rendering orientation of the map, as specified in the TMX `orientation` attribute.  # Variants - `Orthogonal` — Orthogonal variant. - `Isometric` — Isometric variant. - `Staggered` — Staggered variant. - `Hexagonal` — Hexagonal variant.

items.name: TmxStaggerAxis
items.description: The axis along which isometric / hexagonal tiles are staggered.  # Variants - `X` — X variant. - `Y` — Y variant.

items.name: TmxTileset
items.description: A tileset reference embedded in a TMX map.  # Fields - `first_gid` — `u32`. - `source` — `Option<String>`. - `name` — `String`. - `tile_width` — `u32`. - `tile_height` — `u32`. - `spacing` — `u32`. - `margin` — `u32`. - `tile_count` — `u32`. - `columns` — `u32`. - `image_source` — `Option<String>`. - `image_width` — `u32`. - `image_height` — `u32`. - `solid_tiles` — `Vec<u32>`.  If `source` is `Some`, the tileset data lives in an external `.tsx` file and only `first_gid` is available here.  If `source` is `None` the tileset is inline and all other fields are populated.

items.name: TmxTileLayer
items.description: A standard tile layer from a TMX map.  # Fields - `name` — `String`. - `width` — `u32`. - `height` — `u32`. - `visible` — `bool`. - `opacity` — `f32`. - `offset_x` — `f32`. - `offset_y` — `f32`. - `tiles` — `Vec<u32>`.

items.name: TmxObjectLayer
items.description: An object layer (object group) from a TMX map.  # Fields - `name` — `String`. - `visible` — `bool`. - `objects` — `Vec<TmxObject>`.

items.name: TmxObject
items.description: A single Tiled object within an object layer.  # Fields - `id` — `u32`. - `name` — `String`. - `obj_type` — `String`. - `x` — `f32`. - `y` — `f32`. - `width` — `f32`. - `height` — `f32`. - `gid` — `u32`.

items.name: TmxLayer
items.description: Variant tag for TMX map layers.  # Variants - `Tile` — Tile variant. - `Object` — Object variant.

items.name: TmxMap
items.description: A fully-parsed TMX map.  # Fields - `width` — `u32`. - `height` — `u32`. - `tile_width` — `u32`. - `tile_height` — `u32`. - `orientation` — `TmxOrientation`. - `stagger_axis` — `Option<TmxStaggerAxis>`. - `hex_side_length` — `u32`. - `tilesets` — `Vec<TmxTileset>`. - `layers` — `Vec<TmxLayer>`. - `background_color` — `Option<[u8; 4]>`.

items.name: load_tmx
items.description: Parses a TMX file given its XML content as a string.  # Parameters - `xml` — `&str`.  # Returns `Result<TmxMap, String>`.  Returns a fully-populated [`TmxMap`], or an error string describing the problem.  ### Tile data encoding - **XML**: `<tile gid="…"/>` child elements — always supported. - **CSV**: Comma-separated list of GIDs — supported without extra dependencies. - **Base64**: Base64-encoded little-endian `u32` array — supported via the `base64` crate. - **Base64 + zlib**: Further decompressed with `flate2` — supported. - **Base64 + gzip**: Gzip-decompressed with `flate2` — supported. - **Base64 + zstd**: Not supported (returns an error).

items.name: Clock
items.description: Tracks per-frame delta time, accumulated total time, and a rolling FPS measurement.

items.name: clock
items.description: Frame-based clock providing delta time, total time, and FPS.

items.name: scheduler
items.description: Scheduled event manager for delayed and repeating timed callbacks.

timer::scheduler.module_doc: Scheduled event manager for delayed and repeating timed callbacks.  Provides [`Scheduler`] for one-shot delays, repeating timers, named events, individual pause/resume, global time-scale, and on-the-fly interval changes.  ## Usage ```rust,no_run # use luna2d::timer::Scheduler; let mut sched = Scheduler::new(); let id = sched.after(2.0);          // fires once after 2 seconds let rid = sched.every(0.5, -1);     // fires every 0.5 s indefinitely sched.set_time_scale(0.5);          // run at half speed let fired = sched.update(0.1);      // advance; returns IDs that fired sched.cancel(rid); ```

items.name: ScheduledEvent
items.description: A single scheduled event with optional name and pause state.  # Fields - `id` — `u32`. - `name` — `Option<String>`. - `remaining` — `f64`. - `interval` — `f64`. - `count` — `i32`. - `one_shot` — `bool`. - `paused` — `bool`.

items.name: Scheduler
items.description: Manages a collection of timed events (one-shot and repeating).  Each event has an integer ID (returned on creation) that can be used for cancellation, pause/resume, and property reads. Named events can also be cancelled by their string name.  The global `time_scale` multiplier compresses or stretches all timers. A scale of `0.5` makes everything run at half speed; `2.0` doubles speed. Individual events can be paused with [`Scheduler::pause`].
